NRSV.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: In the beginning when God created the heavens and the earth,
text.Rotherham: In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.

text.NRSV: the earth was a formless void and darkness covered the face of the deep, while a wind from God swept over the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: Now, the earth, had become waste and wild, and darkness, was on the face of the roaring deep,—but, the Spirit of God, was brooding on the face of the waters,

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let there be light”; and there was light.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Light, be, And light was.

text.NRSV: And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
text.Rotherham: And God saw the light, that it was, good, and God divided the light, from the, darkness;

text.NRSV: God called the light Day, and the darkness he called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.
text.Rotherham: and God called the light, day, but the darkness, called he, night. So it was evening—and it was morning, one day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let there be a dome in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters.”
text.Rotherham: And God said, Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it be a means of dividing, between waters and waters,

text.NRSV: So God made the dome and separated the waters that were under the dome from the waters that were above the dome. And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God made the expanse, and it divided between the waters that were under the expanse and the waters that were above the expanse. And it was so.

text.NRSV: God called the dome Sky. And there was evening and there was morning, the second day.
text.Rotherham: And God called the expanse, heavens. So it was evening—and it was morning, a, second day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the waters under the sky be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together, into one place, and let the dry—ground appear. And it was so.

text.NRSV: God called the dry land Earth, and the waters that were gathered together he called Seas. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God called, the dry-ground, land, but the gathering together of the waters, called he seas. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let the earth put forth vegetation: plants yielding seed, and fruit trees of every kind on earth that bear fruit with the seed in it.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the land put-forth vegetation—herb yielding seed, fruit-tree, bearing fruit after its kind, whose seed is within it on the land. And it was so,

text.NRSV: The earth brought forth vegetation: plants yielding seed of every kind, and trees of every kind bearing fruit with the seed in it. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And the land brought-forth vegetation—herb yielding seed after its kind, and tree bearing fruit, whose seed is within it, after its kind, And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the third day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a third day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let there be lights in the dome of the sky to separate the day from the night; and let them be for signs and for seasons and for days and years,
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let there be luminaries in the expanse of the heavens, to divide between the day and the night,—and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days and years;

text.NRSV: and let them be lights in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: yea let them be for luminaries in the expanse of the heavens, to give light on the earth. And it was so.

text.NRSV: God made the two great lights—the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night—and the stars.
text.Rotherham: And God made the two great luminaries,—the greater luminary to rule the day, and the lesser luminary to rule the night, also the stars.

text.NRSV: God set them in the dome of the sky to give light upon the earth,
text.Rotherham: And God set them in the expanse of the heavens,—to give light on the earth;

text.NRSV: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: and to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the fourth day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a fourth day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the waters bring forth swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the dome of the sky.”
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the waters swarm with an abundance of living soul, and, birds, shall fly over the earth, over the face of the expanse of the heavens.

text.NRSV: So God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves, of every kind, with which the waters swarm, and every winged bird of every kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God created the great sea-monsters,—and every living soul that moveth—[with] which the waters swarmed after their kind and every winged bird—after its kind. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: God blessed them, saying, “Be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God blessed them, saying,—Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let, the birds, multiply in the land.

text.NRSV: And there was evening and there was morning, the fifth day.
text.Rotherham: So it was evening—and it was morning, a fifth day.

text.NRSV: And God said, “Let the earth bring forth living creatures of every kind: cattle and creeping things and wild animals of the earth of every kind.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let the land, bring forth, living soul, after its kind, tame-beast and creeping thing and wild-beast, of the land, after its kind. And it was so.

text.NRSV: God made the wild animals of the earth of every kind, and the cattle of every kind, and everything that creeps upon the ground of every kind. And God saw that it was good.
text.Rotherham: And God made the wild-beast of the land after its kind, and the tame-beast after its kind, and every creeping thing of the ground, after its kind. And God saw that it was good.

text.NRSV: Then God said, “Let us make humankind in our image, according to our likeness; and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the birds of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the wild animals of the earth, and over every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God said—Let us make man in our image after our likeness—and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the bird of the heavens and over the tame-beast—and over all the land, and over every creeping thing, that creepeth on the land.

text.NRSV: So God created humankind in his image, in the image of God he created them; male and female he created them.
text.Rotherham: And God created the man in his own image, In the image of God, created he him,—Male and female, created he, them.

text.NRSV: God blessed them, and God said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth and subdue it; and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the air and over every living thing that moves upon the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God blessed them, and God said to them Be fruitful and multiply and fill the earth and subdue it,—and have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the bird of the heavens, and over every living thing that moveth on the land.

text.NRSV: God said, “See, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree with seed in its fruit; you shall have them for food.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Lo! I have given to you—every herb yielding seed which is on the face of all the land, and every tree wherein is the fruit of a tree yielding seed,—to you, shall it be, for food;

text.NRSV: And to every beast of the earth, and to every bird of the air, and to everything that creeps on the earth, everything that has the breath of life, I have given every green plant for food.” And it was so.
text.Rotherham: and to every living thing of the land—and to every bird of the heavens, and to every thing that moveth on the land, wherein is a living soul, every green herb for food. And it was so.

text.NRSV: God saw everything that he had made, and indeed, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
text.Rotherham: And God saw every thing which he had made, and lo! it was very good. So it was evening—and it was morning, the sixth day.

text.NRSV: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all their multitude.
text.Rotherham: Thus were finished the heavens and the earth and all their host.

text.NRSV: And on the seventh day God finished the work that he had done, and he rested on the seventh day from all the work that he had done.
text.Rotherham: Thus God finished, on the seventh day his work which he had made, and rested, on the seventh day, from all his work which he had made.

text.NRSV: So God blessed the seventh day and hallowed it, because on it God rested from all the work that he had done in creation.
text.Rotherham: And God blessed the seventh day, and hallowed it,—because therein, rested he from all his work which God, by creating, had made.

text.NRSV: These are the generations of the heavens and the earth when they were created. In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens,
text.Rotherham: These are the geneses of the heavens and the earth when they were created,—in the day when Yahweh God made earth and heavens.

text.NRSV: when no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up—for the Lord God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no one to till the ground;
text.Rotherham: Now, no bush of the field, as yet—was in the earth, and, no herb of the field, as yet had sprung up,—because Yahweh God had not sent rain on the earth, and, man, was there none to till the ground;

text.NRSV: but a stream would rise from the earth, and water the whole face of the ground—
text.Rotherham: but, a vapour, went up from the earth,—and watered all the face of the ground.

text.NRSV: then the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and the man became a living being.
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed in his nostrils the breath of life—and man became a living soul.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God planted a garden in Eden, in the east; and there he put the man whom he had formed.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God planted a garden in Eden, on the east,—and put there the man whom he had formed.

text.NRSV: Out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight and good for food, the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God, caused to spring up, out of the ground, every tree pleasant to the sight and good for food,—and the tree of life, in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.

text.NRSV: A river flows out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divides and becomes four branches.
text.Rotherham: Now, a river, was coming forth out of Eden, to water the garden,—and, from thence, it parted, and became four heads,

text.NRSV: The name of the first is Pishon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
text.Rotherham: The name of the one, is Pishon,—the same, is that which surroundeth all the land of Havilah, where is gold;

text.NRSV: and the gold of that land is good; bdellium and onyx stone are there.
text.Rotherham: moreover the gold of that land, is good,—there, is the bdellium, and the beryl stone,

text.NRSV: The name of the second river is Gihon; it is the one that flows around the whole land of Cush.
text.Rotherham: And the name of the second river, is Gihon,—the same, is that which surroundeth all the land of Cush,

text.NRSV: The name of the third river is Tigris, which flows east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
text.Rotherham: And, the name of the third river, is Hiddekel, the same, is that which goeth in front of Assyria; and the fourth river, is Euphrates.

text.NRSV: The Lord God took the man and put him in the garden of Eden to till it and keep it.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God took the man,—and placed him in the garden of Eden, to till it and to keep it.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God commanded the man, “You may freely eat of every tree of the garden;
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God laid command on the man, saying,—Of every tree of the garden, thou mayest eat;

text.NRSV: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall die.”
text.Rotherham: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it,—for in the day thou eatest thereof, thou shalt die.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said, “It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper as his partner.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God said, It is, not good, that the man should remain alone,—I will make for him a helper as his counterpart.

text.NRSV: So out of the ground the Lord God formed every animal of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called every living creature, that was its name.
text.Rotherham: Now Yahweh God had formed from the ground every living thing of the field and every bird of the heavens, which he brought in unto the man, that he might see what he should call it,—and, whatsoever the man should call it—any living soul, that, should be the name thereof.

text.NRSV: The man gave names to all cattle, and to the birds of the air, and to every animal of the field; but for the man there was not found a helper as his partner.
text.Rotherham: So the man gave names to all the tame-beasts, and to the birds of the heavens, and to all the wild-beasts of the field,—but, for man, had there not been found a helper as his counterpart.

text.NRSV: So the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; then he took one of his ribs and closed up its place with flesh.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God caused a deep sleep to fall on the man, and he slept,—and he took one of his ribs, and closed up flesh instead thereof.

text.NRSV: And the rib that the Lord God had taken from the man he made into a woman and brought her to the man.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God builded the rib which he had taken from the man, into a woman, and brought her in unto the man.

text.NRSV: Then the man said, “This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; this one shall be called Woman, for out of Man this one was taken.”
text.Rotherham: And the man said, This, one, now, is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh! This, one shall be called Woman, for, out of a man, hath this one been taken.

text.NRSV: Therefore a man leaves his father and his mother and clings to his wife, and they become one flesh.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, will a man leave his father and his mother, and cleave unto his wife, and they shall become one flesh.

text.NRSV: And the man and his wife were both naked, and were not ashamed.
text.Rotherham: And they were both of them naked, the man and his wife,—and put not each other to shame.

text.NRSV: Now the serpent was more crafty than any other wild animal that the Lord God had made. He said to the woman, “Did God say, ‘You shall not eat from any tree in the garden’?”
text.Rotherham: Now, the serpent, was more crafty than any living thing of the field which Yahweh God had made, so he said unto the woman, Can it really be that God hath said, Ye shall not eat of every tree of the garden?

text.NRSV: The woman said to the serpent, “We may eat of the fruit of the trees in the garden;
text.Rotherham: And the woman said unto the serpent,—Of the fruit of the trees of the garden, we may eat;

text.NRSV: but God said, ‘You shall not eat of the fruit of the tree that is in the middle of the garden, nor shall you touch it, or you shall die.’”
text.Rotherham: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said Ye shall not eat of it neither shall ye touch it,—lest ye die.

text.NRSV: But the serpent said to the woman, “You will not die;
text.Rotherham: And the serpent said unto the woman,—Ye shall not die,

text.NRSV: for God knows that when you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.”
text.Rotherham: For God doth know, that in the day ye eat thereof, then shall your eyes be opened,—and ye shall become like God, knowing good and evil.

text.NRSV: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was a delight to the eyes, and that the tree was to be desired to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate; and she also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate.
text.Rotherham: And, when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, and that it was desirable to the eyes and the tree was pleasant to make one knowing, then took she of the fruit thereof, and did eat, and she gave to her husband also, along with her, and he did eat.

text.NRSV: Then the eyes of both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together and made loincloths for themselves.
text.Rotherham: Then were opened the eyes of them both, and they knew that, naked, they were,—so they tacked together fig-leaves, and made for themselves girdles,

text.NRSV: They heard the sound of the Lord God walking in the garden at the time of the evening breeze, and the man and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the Lord God among the trees of the garden.
text.Rotherham: Then heard they the sound of Yahweh God, walking to and fro in the garden at the breeze of the day,—so he hid himself—the man with his wife, from the face of Yahweh God, amid the trees of the garden.

text.NRSV: But the Lord God called to the man, and said to him, “Where are you?”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God called unto the man,—and said to him, Where art thou?

text.NRSV: He said, “I heard the sound of you in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, The sound of thee, heard I in the garden, and I was afraid, for, naked, was I, so I hid myself.

text.NRSV: He said, “Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree of which I commanded you not to eat?”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Who told thee that, naked, thou wast? Of the tree whereof I commanded thee not to eat, hast thou eaten?

text.NRSV: The man said, “The woman whom you gave to be with me, she gave me fruit from the tree, and I ate.”
text.Rotherham: And the man said,—The woman whom thou didst put with me, she, gave me of the tree, so I did eat.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “What is this that you have done?” The woman said, “The serpent tricked me, and I ate.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God to the woman, What is this that thou hast done? And the woman said, the serpent, deceived me, so I did eat.

text.NRSV: The Lord God said to the serpent, “Because you have done this, cursed are you among all animals and among all wild creatures; upon your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God unto the serpent—Because thou hast done this, Accursed, art thou above every tame-beast, and above every wild-beast of the field,—on thy belly, shalt thou go, and dust, shalt thou eat all the days of thy life.

text.NRSV: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; he will strike your head, and you will strike his heel.”
text.Rotherham: And enmity, will I put between thee, and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed,—He shall crush thy head, but, thou, shalt crush his heel.

text.NRSV: To the woman he said, “I will greatly increase your pangs in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children, yet your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.”
text.Rotherham: Unto the woman, he said, I will, increase, thy pain of pregnancy, In pain, shalt thou bear children,—Yet, unto thy husband, shall be thy longing, Though, he, rule over thee.

text.NRSV: And to the man he said, “Because you have listened to the voice of your wife, and have eaten of the tree about which I commanded you, ‘You shall not eat of it,’ cursed is the ground because of you; in toil you shall eat of it all the days of your life;
text.Rotherham: And, to the man, he said, Because thou didst hearken to the voice of thy wife, and so didst eat of the tree as to which I commanded thee, saying, Thou shalt not eat of it, Accursed be the ground for thy sake, In pain, shalt thou eat of it, all the days of thy life;

text.NRSV: thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you; and you shall eat the plants of the field.
text.Rotherham: Thorn also and thistle, shall it shoot forth to thee,—when thou hast come to eat of the herb of the field:

text.NRSV: By the sweat of your face you shall eat bread until you return to the ground, for out of it you were taken; you are dust, and to dust you shall return.”
text.Rotherham: In the sweat of thy face, shalt thou eat bread, until thou return to the ground, because therefrom, wast thou taken,—For, dust, thou art, And, unto dust, shalt thou return.

text.NRSV: The man named his wife Eve, because she was the mother of all living.
text.Rotherham: So the man called the name of his wife, Eve,—in that, she, was made mother of every one living.

text.NRSV: And the Lord God made garments of skins for the man and for his wife, and clothed them.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh God made for the man—and for his wife—tunics of skin and clothed them.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord God said, “See, the man has become like one of us, knowing good and evil; and now, he might reach out his hand and take also from the tree of life, and eat, and live forever”—
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh God—Lo! man, hath become like one of us, in respect of knowing good and evil,—Now, therefore, lest he thrust forth his hand, and take even of the tree of life, and eat, and live to times age-abiding,—

text.NRSV: therefore the Lord God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from which he was taken.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh God put him forth from the garden of Eden,—to till the ground wherefrom he had been taken.

text.NRSV: He drove out the man; and at the east of the garden of Eden he placed the cherubim, and a sword flaming and turning to guard the way to the tree of life.
text.Rotherham: So he expelled the man,—and caused to dwell—in front of the garden of Eden—cherubim and a brandishing sword—flame, to keep the way to the tree of life.

text.NRSV: Now the man knew his wife Eve, and she conceived and bore Cain, saying, “I have produced a man with the help of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the man, having come to know Eve his wife,—she conceived and bare Cain, and said, I have gotten a Man, even Yahweh!

text.NRSV: Next she bore his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep, and Cain a tiller of the ground.
text.Rotherham: And she went on to bear his brother, Abel,—and Abel became a feeder of sheep, whereas Cain, was a tiller of the round.

text.NRSV: In the course of time Cain brought to the Lord an offering of the fruit of the ground,
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass, after certain days, that Cain brought in of the fruit of the ground, a present to Yahweh:

text.NRSV: and Abel for his part brought of the firstlings of his flock, their fat portions. And the Lord had regard for Abel and his offering,
text.Rotherham: Abel, also, even, he, brought in of the firstlings of his sheep, and of their fat, and Yahweh approved of Abel and of his present;

text.NRSV: but for Cain and his offering he had no regard. So Cain was very angry, and his countenance fell.
text.Rotherham: but of Cain and his present, he approved not,—and it angered Cain greatly, and his countenance fell.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Cain, “Why are you angry, and why has your countenance fallen?
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh said unto Cain,—Wherefore hath it angered thee, and wherefore hath thy countenance fallen?

text.NRSV: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is lurking at the door; its desire is for you, but you must master it.”
text.Rotherham: Shall it not, if thou do right, be lifted up? But if thou do not right, at the entrance a sin-bearer is lying,—Unto thee, moreover, shall be his longing, though, thou, rule over him.

text.NRSV: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let us go out to the field.” And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against his brother Abel, and killed him.
text.Rotherham: And Cain said unto Abel his brother Let us go into the field And it came to pass when they were in the field that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?” He said, “I do not know; am I my brother’s keeper?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto Cain, Where is Abel thy brother? And he said I know not, the keeper of my brother, am, I?

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “What have you done? Listen; your brother’s blood is crying out to me from the ground!
text.Rotherham: And he said, What hast thou done? With a voice, the shed-blood of thy brother is crying out to me from the ground,

text.NRSV: And now you are cursed from the ground, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother’s blood from your hand.
text.Rotherham: Now therefore, accursed, art thou,—from the ground which hath opened her mouth, to receive the shed-blood of thy brother at thy hand.

text.NRSV: When you till the ground, it will no longer yield to you its strength; you will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Though thou till the ground, it shall not go on to give its vigour to thee. A wanderer and a fugitive, shalt thou be in the earth.

text.NRSV: Cain said to the Lord, “My punishment is greater than I can bear!
text.Rotherham: And Cain said unto Yahweh—Greater is my punishment than I can bear.

text.NRSV: Today you have driven me away from the soil, and I shall be hidden from your face; I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and anyone who meets me may kill me.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! thou has driven me out, this day, from off the face of the ground And from thy face, shall I be hid, So shall I become a wanderer and a fugitive in the earth. And it shall come to pass, whosoever findeth me, will slay me.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Not so! Whoever kills Cain will suffer a sevenfold vengeance.” And the Lord put a mark on Cain, so that no one who came upon him would kill him.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said to him—Not so, whosoever slayeth Cain seven—fold, shall it be avenged. So Yahweh set, for Cain, a sign, that none finding him should smite him.

text.NRSV: Then Cain went away from the presence of the Lord, and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
text.Rotherham: So Cain went forth from the presence of Yahweh,—and dwelt in the land of Nod, eastward of Eden.

text.NRSV: Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch; and he built a city, and named it Enoch after his son Enoch.
text.Rotherham: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceived, and bare Enoch,—Now it happened that he was building a city, so he called the name of the city, after the name of his son, Enoch.

text.NRSV: To Enoch was born Irad; and Irad was the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael the father of Methushael, and Methushael the father of Lamech.
text.Rotherham: And there was born to Enoch Irad, and, Irad, begat Mehujael,—and Mehujael, begat Methusael,—and Methusael, begat Lamech.

text.NRSV: Lamech took two wives; the name of the one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
text.Rotherham: And Lamech took to himself two wives,—the name of the one, was Adah, and, the name of the second Zillah.

text.NRSV: Adah bore Jabal; he was the ancestor of those who live in tents and have livestock.
text.Rotherham: And Adah bare Jabal,—he, was father of such as dwell in tents, and have cattle;

text.NRSV: His brother’s name was Jubal; he was the ancestor of all those who play the lyre and pipe.
text.Rotherham: and, the name of his brother, was Jubal,—he, was father of everyone handling lyre and flute,

text.NRSV: Zillah bore Tubal-cain, who made all kinds of bronze and iron tools. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
text.Rotherham: And, as for Zillah, she also, bare Tubal-Cain, a sharpener of every cutting instrument of bronze and iron,—and, the sister of Tubal-Cain, was Naamah.

text.NRSV: Lamech said to his wives: “Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; you wives of Lamech, listen to what I say: I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for striking me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Lamech to his wives, Adah and Zillah! hear ye my voice, Ye wives of Lamech! give ear to my tale, For a man, have I slain in dealing my wounds, Yea, a youth, in smiting my blows:

text.NRSV: If Cain is avenged sevenfold, truly Lamech seventy-sevenfold.”
text.Rotherham: If, sevenfold, be, the avenging of Cain, Then, of Lamech, seventy and seven.

text.NRSV: Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, for she said, “God has appointed for me another child instead of Abel, because Cain killed him.”
text.Rotherham: And Adam again knew his wife, and she bare a son, and called his name Seth,—For God hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel, because Cain slew him!

text.NRSV: To Seth also a son was born, and he named him Enosh. At that time people began to invoke the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And to Seth—to him also, was born a son, and he called his name Enosh,—then, was a beginning made, to call on the name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: This is the list of the descendants of Adam. When God created humankind, he made them in the likeness of God.
text.Rotherham: This, is the record of the generations of Adam. In the day when God created man, In the likeness of God, made he him:

text.NRSV: Male and female he created them, and he blessed them and named them “Humankind” when they were created.
text.Rotherham: Male and female, created he them,—and blessed them, and called their name Adam, in the day they were created.

text.NRSV: When Adam had lived one hundred thirty years, he became the father of a son in his likeness, according to his image, and named him Seth.
text.Rotherham: And Adam lived a hundred and thirty years, and begat [a son] in his likeness after his image,—and called his name Seth:

text.NRSV: The days of Adam after he became the father of Seth were eight hundred years; and he had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and the days of Adam, after he begat Seth, were eight hundred years, and he begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: Thus all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred thirty years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: So all the days of Adam which he lived, were nine hundred and thirty years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Seth had lived one hundred five years, he became the father of Enosh.
text.Rotherham: And Seth lived a hundred and five years,—and begat Enosh:

text.NRSV: Seth lived after the birth of Enosh eight hundred seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Seth lived, after he begat Enosh, eight hundred and seven years,—and begat sons and daughters:

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Seth were nine hundred twelve years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Seth were, nine hundred and I twelve years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Enosh had lived ninety years, he became the father of Kenan.
text.Rotherham: And Enosh lived ninety years,—and begat Kenan;

text.NRSV: Enosh lived after the birth of Kenan eight hundred fifteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Enosh lived after he begat Kenan, eight hundred and fifteen years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Enosh were nine hundred five years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Kenan had lived seventy years, he became the father of Mahalalel.
text.Rotherham: And Kenan, lived seventy years,—and begat Mahalalel;

text.NRSV: Kenan lived after the birth of Mahalalel eight hundred and forty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Kenan lived after he begat Mahalalel, eight hundred and forty years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Kenan were, nine hundred and ten years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Mahalalel had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Jared.
text.Rotherham: And Mahalalel lived sixty-five years,—and begat Jared;

text.NRSV: Mahalalel lived after the birth of Jared eight hundred thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Mahalalel lived after he begat Jared, eight hundred and thirty years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred ninety-five years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Mahalalel were, eight hundred and ninety-five years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Jared had lived one hundred sixty-two years he became the father of Enoch.
text.Rotherham: And Jared lived, a hundred and sixty-two years, and begat Enoch;

text.NRSV: Jared lived after the birth of Enoch eight hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Jared lived after he begat Enoch, eight hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Jared were nine hundred sixty-two years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Jared were, nine hundred and sixty-two years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Enoch had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Methuselah.
text.Rotherham: And Enoch lived sixty-five years,—and begat Methuselah;

text.NRSV: Enoch walked with God after the birth of Methuselah three hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Enoch walked with God, after he begat Methuselah, three hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Enoch were three hundred sixty-five years.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Enoch were, three hundred and sixty-five years;

text.NRSV: Enoch walked with God; then he was no more, because God took him.
text.Rotherham: and Enoch walked with God,—and was not for God had taken him.

text.NRSV: When Methuselah had lived one hundred eighty-seven years, he became the father of Lamech.
text.Rotherham: And Methuselah lived, a hundred and eighty-seven years,—and begat Lamech;

text.NRSV: Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech seven hundred eighty-two years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Methuselah lived, after he begat Lamech, seven hundred and eighty-two years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred sixty-nine years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Methuselah were, nine hundred and sixty-nine years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: When Lamech had lived one hundred eighty-two years, he became the father of a son;
text.Rotherham: And Lamech lived, a hundred and eighty-two years,—and begat a son;

text.NRSV: he named him Noah, saying, “Out of the ground that the Lord has cursed this one shall bring us relief from our work and from the toil of our hands.”
text.Rotherham: and he called his name Noah saying,—This, one shall give us rest from our work, And from the grievous toil of our hands, By reason of the ground which Yahweh hath cursed,

text.NRSV: Lamech lived after the birth of Noah five hundred ninety-five years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: And Lamech lived, after he begat Noah, five hundred and ninety-five years,—and begat sons and daughters;

text.NRSV: Thus all the days of Lamech were seven hundred seventy-seven years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: and all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: After Noah was five hundred years old, Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.Rotherham: And Noah was five hundred years old,—and Noah begat Shem, Ham and Japheth.

text.NRSV: When people began to multiply on the face of the ground, and daughters were born to them,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when men had begun to multiply on the face of the ground and daughters had been born to them,

text.NRSV: the sons of God saw that they were fair; and they took wives for themselves of all that they chose.
text.Rotherham: that the sons of God saw the daughters of men! that they were, fair,—so they took to themselves wives of whomsoever they chose,

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “My spirit shall not abide in mortals forever, for they are flesh; their days shall be one hundred twenty years.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—My spirit shall not rule in man to times age—abiding, for that, he also, is flesh,—Yet his days shall be a hundred and twenty years.

text.NRSV: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days—and also afterward—when the sons of God went in to the daughters of humans, who bore children to them. These were the heroes that were of old, warriors of renown.
text.Rotherham: The giants, were in the earth in those days, and also, after that, when the sons of God began to go in unto the daughters of men, and sons were born to them, the same, were the heroes that were from age-past times the men of renown.

text.NRSV: The Lord saw that the wickedness of humankind was great in the earth, and that every inclination of the thoughts of their hearts was only evil continually.
text.Rotherham: Then Yahweh saw that great, was the wickedness of man in the earth, and that, every purpose of the devices of his heart, was only wicked all the day;

text.NRSV: And the Lord was sorry that he had made humankind on the earth, and it grieved him to his heart.
text.Rotherham: and it grieved Yahweh, that he had made man in the earth,—and he took sorrow unto his heart.

text.NRSV: So the Lord said, “I will blot out from the earth the human beings I have created—people together with animals and creeping things and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said—I must wipe off man whom I created from off the face of the ground, from man unto beast unto creeping thing, and unto the bird of the heavens,—for I am grieved that I made them.

text.NRSV: But Noah found favor in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, Noah, had found favour in the eyes, of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation; Noah walked with God.
text.Rotherham: These, are the generations of Noah, Noah, was, a righteous man blameless, in his generations,—with God, did Noah walk.

text.NRSV: And Noah had three sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
text.Rotherham: And Noah begat three sons,—Shem, Ham and Japheth.

text.NRSV: Now the earth was corrupt in God's sight, and the earth was filled with violence.
text.Rotherham: And the earth corrupted itself before God,—and the earth was filled with violence,

text.NRSV: And God saw that the earth was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted its ways upon the earth.
text.Rotherham: And God beheld the earth, and lo! it had corrupted itself,—surely all flesh had corrupted its way, on the earth.

text.NRSV: And God said to Noah, “I have determined to make an end of all flesh, for the earth is filled with violence because of them; now I am going to destroy them along with the earth.
text.Rotherham: So God said unto Noah: the end of all flesh, hath come in before me, for, filled, is the earth with violence, because of them,—behold me, then, destroying them with the earth.

text.NRSV: Make yourself an ark of cypress wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and out with pitch.
text.Rotherham: Make for thee an ark of timbers of gopher, rooms, shalt thou make with the ark,—and thou shalt cover it within and without with pitch.

text.NRSV: This is how you are to make it: the length of the ark three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is how thou shalt make it,—three hundred cubits, the length of the ark, fifty cubits, the breadth thereof, and thirty cubits the height thereof.

text.NRSV: Make a roof for the ark, and finish it to a cubit above; and put the door of the ark in its side; make it with lower, second, and third decks.
text.Rotherham: A place for light, shalt thou make to the ark and to a cubit, shalt thou finish it upwards, and the opening of the ark—in the side thereof, shalt thou put,—with lower, second and third stories, shalt thou make it.

text.NRSV: For my part, I am going to bring a flood of waters on the earth, to destroy from under heaven all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall die.
text.Rotherham: And, I, behold me! bringing in the flood—even waters upon the earth, to destroy all flesh wherein is the spirit of life, from under the heavens,—everything that is in the earth, shall cease to breathe:

text.NRSV: But I will establish my covenant with you; and you shall come into the ark, you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives with you.
text.Rotherham: therefore will I establish my covenant with thee,—and thou shalt enter into the ark, thou, and thy sons, and thy wife, and thy sons’ wives, with thee.

text.NRSV: And of every living thing, of all flesh, you shall bring two of every kind into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.
text.Rotherham: Moreover of all the living creatures of all flesh—two of each, shalt thou bring into the ark to keep alive with thee,—male and female, shall they be.

text.NRSV: Of the birds according to their kinds, and of the animals according to their kinds, of every creeping thing of the ground according to its kind, two of every kind shall come in to you, to keep them alive.
text.Rotherham: Of the bird after its kind and of the beast after its kind, [and] of every creeping thing of the ground after its kind, two of each, shall come in unto thee. for keeping alive.

text.NRSV: Also take with you every kind of food that is eaten, and store it up; and it shall serve as food for you and for them.”
text.Rotherham: But thou, take to thee of all food that is eaten, and gather it unto thee,—and it shall be for thee and for them for food.

text.NRSV: Noah did this; he did all that God commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Noah did [so],—according to all that God commanded him, so did he.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Noah, “Go into the ark, you and all your household, for I have seen that you alone are righteous before me in this generation.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said to Noah, Enter thou and all thy house, into the ark,—for, thee, have I seen righteous before me, in this generation.

text.NRSV: Take with you seven pairs of all clean animals, the male and its mate; and a pair of the animals that are not clean, the male and its mate;
text.Rotherham: Of all the clean beasts, shalt thou take to thee by sevens, a male and his female,—and, of the beasts that are not clean, shall be two a male and his female.

text.NRSV: and seven pairs of the birds of the air also, male and female, to keep their kind alive on the face of all the earth.
text.Rotherham: Also of the bird of the heavens, by sevens, male and female,—to keep alive a seed on the face of all the earth.

text.NRSV: For in seven days I will send rain on the earth for forty days and forty nights; and every living thing that I have made I will blot out from the face of the ground.”
text.Rotherham: For in seven days more, I, am sending rain on the earth, forty days and forty nights,—so will I wipe out all the living things which I have made, from off the face of the ground.

text.NRSV: And Noah did all that the Lord had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Noah did,—according to all that Yahweh commanded him.

text.NRSV: Noah was six hundred years old when the flood of waters came on the earth.
text.Rotherham: Now, Noah, was six hundred years old,—when, the flood, came, even waters on the earth.

text.NRSV: And Noah with his sons and his wife and his sons' wives went into the ark to escape the waters of the flood.
text.Rotherham: So Noah entered, and his sons and his wife and his sons’ wives with him into the ark,—from before the waters of the flood,

text.NRSV: Of clean animals, and of animals that are not clean, and of birds, and of everything that creeps on the ground,
text.Rotherham: Of the beasts that were clean, and of the beasts that were not clean,—and of the birds, and everything that creepeth on the ground,

text.NRSV: two and two, male and female, went into the ark with Noah, as God had commanded Noah.
text.Rotherham: two and two, went they in unto Noah, into the ark male and female,—as God commanded Noah.

text.NRSV: And after seven days the waters of the flood came on the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the seven days,—that the waters of the flood, came on the earth.

text.NRSV: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, on that day all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the windows of the heavens were opened.
text.Rotherham: In the six hundredth year, the year of the life of Noah. in the second month on the seventeenth day of the month on this day, were burst open all the fountains of the great roaring deep, and the windows of the heavens, were set open.

text.NRSV: The rain fell on the earth forty days and forty nights.
text.Rotherham: (And it came to pass that the heavy rain was on the earth,—forty days and forty nights.)

text.NRSV: On the very same day Noah with his sons, Shem and Ham and Japheth, and Noah's wife and the three wives of his sons entered the ark,
text.Rotherham: On this selfsame day, entered Noah, and Shem and Ham and Japheth. Noah’s sons,—and Noah’s wife, and his sons’ three wives with them into the ark:

text.NRSV: they and every wild animal of every kind, and all domestic animals of every kind, and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth, and every bird of every kind—every bird, every winged creature.
text.Rotherham: they, and all the wild-beasts after their kind and all the tame-beasts after their kind, and all the creeping things that creep on the earth after their kind,—and all the birds after their kind, every bird of every wing.

text.NRSV: They went into the ark with Noah, two and two of all flesh in which there was the breath of life.
text.Rotherham: So they entered in unto Noah, into the ark,—two and two of all flesh, wherein was the spirit of life.

text.NRSV: And those that entered, male and female of all flesh, went in as God had commanded him; and the Lord shut him in.
text.Rotherham: And, they that entered, male and female of all flesh, entered, as God commanded him,—and Yahweh shut him in round about.

text.NRSV: The flood continued forty days on the earth; and the waters increased, and bore up the ark, and it rose high above the earth.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that the flood was forty days on the earth,—and the waters increased and bare up the ark, and it was lifted high above the earth,

text.NRSV: The waters swelled and increased greatly on the earth; and the ark floated on the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: And the waters prevailed and increased greatly, on the earth,—and the ark went its way on the face of the waters.

text.NRSV: The waters swelled so mightily on the earth that all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered;
text.Rotherham: Yea the waters, prevailed very greatly, on the earth,—so that all the high mountains became covered, that were under all the heavens:

text.NRSV: the waters swelled above the mountains, covering them fifteen cubits deep.
text.Rotherham: fifteen cubits upwards, prevailed the waters, so that the mountains became covered.

text.NRSV: And all flesh died that moved on the earth, birds, domestic animals, wild animals, all swarming creatures that swarm on the earth, and all human beings;
text.Rotherham: And all flesh ceased to breathe that moved on the earth, of birds and of tame-beasts and of wild-beasts, and of all the swarming things that swarm on the earth,—and all mankind.

text.NRSV: everything on dry land in whose nostrils was the breath of life died.
text.Rotherham: All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life of all that were on the dry ground died.

text.NRSV: He blotted out every living thing that was on the face of the ground, human beings and animals and creeping things and birds of the air; they were blotted out from the earth. Only Noah was left, and those that were with him in the ark.
text.Rotherham: Thus was wiped out all that existed on the face of the ground, from man unto beast unto creeping thing, and unto the bird of the heavens, thus were they wiped out from the earth,—so that there was left—only Noah and they that were with him in the ark.

text.NRSV: And the waters swelled on the earth for one hundred fifty days.
text.Rotherham: Thus prevailed the waters on the earth,—a hundred and fifty days.

text.NRSV: But God remembered Noah and all the wild animals and all the domestic animals that were with him in the ark. And God made a wind blow over the earth, and the waters subsided;
text.Rotherham: And God remembered Noah, and all the wild—beasts and all the tame-beasts that were with him in the ark,—and God caused a wind to pass over the earth, and the waters subsided;

text.NRSV: the fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed, the rain from the heavens was restrained,
text.Rotherham: and the fountains of the roaring-deep were shut, and the windows of the heavens,—and the heavy rain was restrained from the heavens;

text.NRSV: and the waters gradually receded from the earth. At the end of one hundred fifty days the waters had abated;
text.Rotherham: and the waters returned from off the earth they went on returning,—and so the waters decreased at the end of a hundred and fifty days.

text.NRSV: and in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
text.Rotherham: And the ark rested, in the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month,—on the mountains of Ararat.

text.NRSV: The waters continued to abate until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains appeared.
text.Rotherham: But, the waters went on decreasing, until the tenth month,—in the tenth [month], on the first of the month, were seen the tops of the mountains.

text.NRSV: At the end of forty days Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at the end of forty days, that Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made;

text.NRSV: and sent out the raven; and it went to and fro until the waters were dried up from the earth.
text.Rotherham: and sent forth a raven,—and it kept going forth and returning, until the drying up of the waters from off the earth.

text.NRSV: Then he sent out the dove from him, to see if the waters had subsided from the face of the ground;
text.Rotherham: And he sent forth a dove from him,—to see whether the waters had abated, from off the face of the ground;

text.NRSV: but the dove found no place to set its foot, and it returned to him to the ark, for the waters were still on the face of the whole earth. So he put out his hand and took it and brought it into the ark with him.
text.Rotherham: but the dove found no resting-place for the sole of her foot so she returned unto him into the ark, for, waters, were on the face of all the earth; and he put forth his hand and took her, and brought her in unto him, into the ark.

text.NRSV: He waited another seven days, and again he sent out the dove from the ark;
text.Rotherham: Then stayed he yet seven days more,—and, again sent forth the dove out of the ark.

text.NRSV: and the dove came back to him in the evening, and there in its beak was a freshly plucked olive leaf; so Noah knew that the waters had subsided from the earth.
text.Rotherham: And the dove came in unto him at eventide, and lo! a newly sprouted olive-leaf, in her mouth,—so Noah knew that the waters had abated from off the earth.

text.NRSV: Then he waited another seven days, and sent out the dove; and it did not return to him any more.
text.Rotherham: And he stayed yet seven days more,—and sent forth the dove, but she returned not again unto him any more.

text.NRSV: In the six hundred first year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, the waters were dried up from the earth; and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked, and saw that the face of the ground was drying.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass in the six hundred and first year at the beginning, on the first of the month, that the waters had dried up from off the earth,—and Noah removed the covering of the ark, and looked and lo! the face of the ground was dried.

text.NRSV: In the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was dry.
text.Rotherham: And in the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, was the earth dry.

text.NRSV: Then God said to Noah,
text.Rotherham: So then, God spake to Noah, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go out of the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.
text.Rotherham: Come forth out of the ark,—thou and thy wife, and thy sons, and thy sons’ wives with thee.

text.NRSV: Bring out with you every living thing that is with you of all flesh—birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth—so that they may abound on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: All the living creatures that are with thee of all flesh among birds, and among beasts, and among all the creeping things that creep on the earth, bring forth with thee,—and they shall swarm in the earth, and be fruitful and multiply, on the earth.

text.NRSV: So Noah went out with his sons and his wife and his sons' wives.
text.Rotherham: So Noah came forth,—and his sons and his wife, and his sons’ wives with him:

text.NRSV: And every animal, every creeping thing, and every bird, everything that moves on the earth, went out of the ark by families.
text.Rotherham: All the living creatures, all that move along, and all birds, and all that moveth along over the earth—by their families, came forth out of the ark.

text.NRSV: Then Noah built an altar to the Lord, and took of every clean animal and of every clean bird, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And Noah builded an altar to Yahweh,—and took of all the clean beasts and of all the clean birds, and caused ascending Sacrifices to go up on the altar.

text.NRSV: And when the Lord smelled the pleasing odor, the Lord said in his heart, “I will never again curse the ground because of humankind, for the inclination of the human heart is evil from youth; nor will I ever again destroy every living creature as I have done.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh smelled a satisfying odour, so Yahweh said to himself. I will not, again, curse any more the ground for man’s sake, although the device of the heart of man, be wicked from his youth,—neither will I again, any more smite every living thing, as I have done.

text.NRSV: As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night, shall not cease.”
text.Rotherham: During all the days of the earth, seedtime and harvest, and cold and heat and summer and winter, and day and night, shall not cease.

text.NRSV: God blessed Noah and his sons, and said to them, “Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth.
text.Rotherham: So God blessed Noah and his sons,—and said to them Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth;

text.NRSV: The fear and dread of you shall rest on every animal of the earth, and on every bird of the air, on everything that creeps on the ground, and on all the fish of the sea; into your hand they are delivered.
text.Rotherham: and let the fear of you and the dread of you be upon every living creature of the earth, and upon every bird of the heavens,—over everything that moveth along on the ground, and over all the fishes of the sea,—into your hand, have they been given.

text.NRSV: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you; and just as I gave you the green plants, I give you everything.
text.Rotherham: As for every moving thing that hath life, yours, shall it be, for food,—Like the green herb, have I given you all things.

text.NRSV: Only, you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood.
text.Rotherham: Yet flesh with the life thereof, the blood thereof, shall ye not eat;

text.NRSV: For your own lifeblood I will surely require a reckoning: from every animal I will require it and from human beings, each one for the blood of another, I will require a reckoning for human life.
text.Rotherham: And surely your blood, of your lives, will I require, From the hand of every living creature, will I require it,—and from the hand of man From the hand of each one’s brother, will I require the life of man:

text.NRSV: Whoever sheds the blood of a human, by a human shall that person's blood be shed; for in his own image God made humankind.
text.Rotherham: He that sheddeth man’s blood, by man, shall his blood be shed,—For, in the image of God, made he man.

text.NRSV: And you, be fruitful and multiply, abound on the earth and multiply in it.”
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, be fruitful and multiply, swarm in the earth and multiply therein.

text.NRSV: Then God said to Noah and to his sons with him,
text.Rotherham: And God spake unto Noah, and unto his sons with him saying:

text.NRSV: “As for me, I am establishing my covenant with you and your descendants after you,
text.Rotherham: I, therefore, behold me! establishing my covenant with you,—and with your seed after you;

text.NRSV: and with every living creature that is with you, the birds, the domestic animals, and every animal of the earth with you, as many as came out of the ark.
text.Rotherham: and with every living soul that is with you, of birds, of tame-beasts and of all wild-beasts of the earth that are with you,—of all coming forth out of the ark, even to all wild-beasts of the earth;

text.NRSV: I establish my covenant with you, that never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of a flood, and never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.”
text.Rotherham: yea, I will establish my covenant with you, so that all flesh shall not be cut off any more, by reason of the waters of a flood,—neither shall there be any more a flood to destroy the earth.

text.NRSV: God said, “This is the sign of the covenant that I make between me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations:
text.Rotherham: And God said, This, is the sign of the covenant which I am granting betwixt me and you, and every living soul that is with you,—to age-abiding generations:—

text.NRSV: I have set my bow in the clouds, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and the earth.
text.Rotherham: My bow, have I set in the cloud,—and it shall be for a sign of a covenant, betwixt me and the earth;

text.NRSV: When I bring clouds over the earth and the bow is seen in the clouds,
text.Rotherham: and it shall be when I draw a veil of cloud over the earth,—and the bow in the cloud appeareth,

text.NRSV: I will remember my covenant that is between me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all flesh.
text.Rotherham: then will I remember my covenant which is betwixt me and you, and every living soul among all flesh,—that the waters may no more become a flood, to destroy all flesh:

text.NRSV: When the bow is in the clouds, I will see it and remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: so the bow shall be in the cloud,—and I will behold it, to remember an age-abiding covenant, between God, and every living soul among all flesh that is on the earth.

text.NRSV: God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant that I have established between me and all flesh that is on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Noah, This is the sign of the covenant which have established, between me and all flesh that is on the earth.

text.NRSV: The sons of Noah who went out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Ham was the father of Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Noah who came forth out of the ark, were—Shem and Ham and Japheth,—now, Ham, was the father of Canaan.

text.NRSV: These three were the sons of Noah; and from these the whole earth was peopled.
text.Rotherham: These three, were the sons of Noah,—and from these, was all the earth overspread.

text.NRSV: Noah, a man of the soil, was the first to plant a vineyard.
text.Rotherham: And Noah began to be a husbandman,—so he planted a vineyard:

text.NRSV: He drank some of the wine and became drunk, and he lay uncovered in his tent.
text.Rotherham: and dank of the wine and became drunk, and uncovered himself in the midst of his tent.

text.NRSV: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father, and told his two brothers outside.
text.Rotherham: And Ham the father of Canaan saw his father’s shame,—and told his two brethren outside.

text.NRSV: Then Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it on both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of his father; their faces were turned away, and they did not see their father's nakedness.
text.Rotherham: And Shem and Japheth took a mantle, and put it on the shoulder of them both and went backwards, and covered the shame of their father, but their faces were backwards, and the shame of their father, saw they not.

text.NRSV: When Noah awoke from his wine and knew what his youngest son had done to him,
text.Rotherham: And Noah awoke from his wine,—and came to know what his youngest son had done to him.

text.NRSV: he said, “Cursed be Canaan; lowest of slaves shall he be to his brothers.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Accursed be Canaan,—a servant of servants, shall he be to his brethren!

text.NRSV: He also said, “Blessed by the Lord my God be Shem; and let Canaan be his slave.
text.Rotherham: And he said, Blessed be Yahweh, God of Shem,—And let Canaan be their servant:

text.NRSV: May God make space for Japheth, and let him live in the tents of Shem; and let Canaan be his slave.”
text.Rotherham: God give extension to Japheth, But make his habitation in the tents of Shem, And let Canaan be their servant.

text.NRSV: After the flood Noah lived three hundred fifty years.
text.Rotherham: And Noah lived after the flood,—three hundred and fifty years:

text.NRSV: All the days of Noah were nine hundred fifty years; and he died.
text.Rotherham: so, all the days of Noah, were—nine hundred and fifty years,—and he died.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Noah's sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth; children were born to them after the flood.
text.Rotherham: Now, these are the generations of the sons of Noah, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and there were born to them sons after the flood.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Japheth: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Japheth: Gomer and Magog, and Madai, and Javan and Tubal,—and Meshech, and Tiras.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Gomer: Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Gomer: Ashkenaz and Riphath, and Togarmah.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Javan: Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Rodanim.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Javan: Elishah, and Tarshish. Kittim, and Rodanim,—

text.NRSV: From these the coastland peoples spread. These are the descendants of Japheth in their lands, with their own language, by their families, in their nations.
text.Rotherham: From these, were dispersed [the inhabitants] of the coastlands of the nations, in their lands, each man by his tongue, by their families in their nations.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Ham: Cush, Egypt, Put, and Canaan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Ham: Cush and Mizraim and Phut and Canaan.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Cush: Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca. The descendants of Raamah: Sheba and Dedan.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Cush: Seba and Havilah, and Sabtah and Raamah, and Sabtechah,—And the sons of Raamah, Sheba and Dedan.

text.NRSV: Cush became the father of Nimrod; he was the first on earth to become a mighty warrior.
text.Rotherham: And, Cush begat Nimrod.—he, became a hero in the earth;

text.NRSV: He was a mighty hunter before the Lord; therefore it is said, “Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: he, became a hero of hunting before Yahweh, for this cause, it is said. Like Nimrod, a hero of hunting before Yahweh.

text.NRSV: The beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, and Accad, all of them in the land of Shinar.
text.Rotherham: So the beginning of his kingdom came to be Babel, and Erech, and Accad and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.

text.NRSV: From that land he went into Assyria, and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-ir, Calah, and
text.Rotherham: From that land, went forth Asshur,—and he built Nineveh, and Rehoboth-ir, and Calah:

text.NRSV: Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.
text.Rotherham: and Resen, between Niveveh and Calah,—the same is the great city.

text.NRSV: Egypt became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
text.Rotherham: And, Mizraim, begat Ludim and Anamim and Lehabim, and Naphtuchim;

text.NRSV: Pathrusim, Casluhim, and Caphtorim, from which the Philistines come.
text.Rotherham: and Pathrusim and Casluhim whence came forth Philistim and Caphtorim.

text.NRSV: Canaan became the father of Sidon his firstborn, and Heth,
text.Rotherham: And Canaan begat Zidon his firstborn, and Heth:

text.NRSV: and the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
text.Rotherham: and the Jebusite, and the Amorite, and the Girgashite;

text.NRSV: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
text.Rotherham: and the Hivite and the Arkite, and the Sinite:

text.NRSV: the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites. Afterward the families of the Canaanites spread abroad.
text.Rotherham: and the Arvadite and the Zemarite and the Hamathite,—and after-wards, were spread abroad the families of the Canaanite.

text.NRSV: And the territory of the Canaanites extended from Sidon, in the direction of Gerar, as far as Gaza, and in the direction of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that the boundary of the Canaanite was from Zidon, as thou enterest in towards Gerar unto Gaza,—as thou enterest in towards Sodom and Gomorrah and Admah and Zeboim, as far as Lasha.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Ham, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
text.Rotherham: These, are the sons of Ham, by their families by their tongues, in their lands in their nations.

text.NRSV: To Shem also, the father of all the children of Eber, the elder brother of Japheth, children were born.
text.Rotherham: Now, to Shem, father of all the sons of Eber, elder brother of Japheth, to him also, were children born.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Shem: Elam, Asshur, Arpachshad, Lud, and Aram.
text.Rotherham: The sons of Shem, Elam and Asshur,—and Arpachshad and Lud and Aram.

text.NRSV: The descendants of Aram: Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Aram: Uz and Hul, and Gether and Mash.

text.NRSV: Arpachshad became the father of Shelah; and Shelah became the father of Eber.
text.Rotherham: Now Arpachshad, begat Shelah, and Shelah begat Eber.

text.NRSV: To Eber were born two sons: the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided, and his brother's name was Joktan.
text.Rotherham: And to Eber, were born two sons,—the name of the one was Peleg, for in his days, was the earth divided, and the name of his brother was Joktan.

text.NRSV: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
text.Rotherham: Now, Joktan, begat Almodad, and Sheleph, and Hazarmaveth, and Jerah;

text.NRSV: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
text.Rotherham: and Hadoram and Uzal, and Diklah:

text.NRSV: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
text.Rotherham: and Obal and Abimael, and Sheba:

text.NRSV: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab; all these were the descendants of Joktan.
text.Rotherham: and Ophir and Havilah, and Jobab—all these were sons of Joktan.

text.NRSV: The territory in which they lived extended from Mesha in the direction of Sephar, the hill country of the east.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that their dwelling was from Mesha, as thou enterest in towards Sephar, the mountain of the east.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Shem, by their families, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
text.Rotherham: These are the sons of Shem, by their families by their tongues, in their lands by their nations.

text.NRSV: These are the families of Noah's sons, according to their genealogies, in their nations; and from these the nations spread abroad on the earth after the flood.
text.Rotherham: These, are the families of the sons of Noah, by their generations in their nations, and from these, were dispersed the nations in the earth after the flood.

text.NRSV: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass that, all the earth, was of one manner of speech,—and of one stock of words.

text.NRSV: And as they migrated from the east, they came upon a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass as they brake away onwards, that they found a plain in the land of Shinar, and dwelt there.

text.NRSV: And they said to one another, “Come, let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly.” And they had brick for stone, and bitumen for mortar.
text.Rotherham: Then said they, each man to his friend, Come on! let us make bricks, and burn them thoroughly,—So the bricks, served them for stone, and bitumen, served them for mortar,

text.NRSV: Then they said, “Come, let us build ourselves a city, and a tower with its top in the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves; otherwise we shall be scattered abroad upon the face of the whole earth.”
text.Rotherham: And they said Come on! let us build for ourselves a city, and a tower with its head in the heavens, so let us make for ourselves a name,—lest we be scattered abroad over the face of all the earth.

text.NRSV: The Lord came down to see the city and the tower, which mortals had built.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh came down, to behold the city, and the tower,—which the sons of men had built.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “Look, they are one people, and they have all one language; and this is only the beginning of what they will do; nothing that they propose to do will now be impossible for them.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh: Lo! one people and one manner of speech, have they all, this, then is what they have begun to do,—now, therefore, nothing will be withholden from them which they may plan to do.

text.NRSV: Come, let us go down, and confuse their language there, so that they will not understand one another’s speech.”
text.Rotherham: Come on! let us go down, and there confuse their speech,—that they may not understand, each man the speech of his friend.

text.NRSV: So the Lord scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they left off building the city.
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh scattered them from thence over the face of all the earth,—and so they left off building the city.

text.NRSV: Therefore it was called Babel, because there the Lord confused the language of all the earth; and from there the Lord scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, was the name thereof called Babel [="Confusion"]; because there, did Yahweh confuse the speech of all the earth,—and, from thence, did Yahweh scatter them abroad, over the face of all the earth.

text.NRSV: These are the descendants of Shem. When Shem was one hundred years old, he became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood;
text.Rotherham: These, are the genealogies of Shem, Shem was a hundred years old when he begat Arpachshad, two years after the flood

text.NRSV: and Shem lived after the birth of Arpachshad five hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Shem lived after he begat Arpachshad, five hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Arpachshad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah;
text.Rotherham: And, Arpachshad, lived thirty-five years,—and begat Shelah;

text.NRSV: and Arpachshad lived after the birth of Shelah four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Arpachshad lived after he begat Shelah, four hundred and three years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Shelah had lived thirty years, he became the father of Eber;
text.Rotherham: And, Shelah, lived thirty years,—and begat Eber;

text.NRSV: and Shelah lived after the birth of Eber four hundred three years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Shelah lived, after he begat Eber, four hundred and three years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Eber had lived thirty-four years, he became the father of Peleg;
text.Rotherham: And Eber lived thirty-four years,—and begat Peleg;

text.NRSV: and Eber lived after the birth of Peleg four hundred thirty years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Eber lived after he begat Peleg, four hundred and thirty years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu;
text.Rotherham: And Peleg lived thirty years, and begat Reu:

text.NRSV: and Peleg lived after the birth of Reu two hundred nine years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Peleg lived after he begat Reu, two hundred and nine years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug;
text.Rotherham: And Reu lived thirty-two years—and begat Serug;

text.NRSV: and Reu lived after the birth of Serug two hundred seven years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Reu lived after he begat Serug, two hundred and seven years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor;
text.Rotherham: And Serug lived thirty years,—and begat Nahor;

text.NRSV: and Serug lived after the birth of Nahor two hundred years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Serug lived after he begat Nahor, two hundred years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Nahor had lived twenty-nine years, he became the father of Terah;
text.Rotherham: And Nahor lived twenty-nine years,—and begat Terah;

text.NRSV: and Nahor lived after the birth of Terah one hundred nineteen years, and had other sons and daughters.
text.Rotherham: and Nahor lived, after he begat Terah, a hundred and nineteen years,—and begat sons and daughters.

text.NRSV: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
text.Rotherham: And Terah lived seventy years,—and begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran.

text.NRSV: Now these are the descendants of Terah. Terah was the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran was the father of Lot.
text.Rotherham: These, then are the generations of Terah, Terah begat Abram, Nahor, and Haran,—and, Haran, begat Lot;

text.NRSV: Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
text.Rotherham: and Haran died, in the presence of Terah his father,—in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldees.

text.NRSV: Abram and Nahor took wives; the name of Abram’s wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor’s wife was Milcah. She was the daughter of Haran the father of Milcah and Iscah.
text.Rotherham: And Abram and Nahor took to themselves wives; the name of Abram’s wife, was Sarai; and, the name of Nahor’s wife, Milcah, daughter of Haran father of Milcah, and father of Iscah.

text.NRSV: Now Sarai was barren; she had no child.
text.Rotherham: And, Sarai, remained barren,—she had no child,

text.NRSV: Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram’s wife, and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
text.Rotherham: And Terah took Abram his son and Lot son of Haran his son’s son, and Sarai his daughter-in-law, wife of Abram his son,—and came forth with them out of Ur of the Chaldees, that they might go their way towards the land of Canaan, and they came in as far as Haran and dwelt there.

text.NRSV: The days of Terah were two hundred five years; and Terah died in Haran.
text.Rotherham: And the days of Terah were two hundred and five years,—and Terah died in Haran.

text.NRSV: Now the Lord said to Abram, “Go from your country and your kindred and your father’s house to the land that I will show you.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Abram: Come thou on thy way, Out of thy land and out of the place of thy birth and out of the house of thy father,—Unto the land that I will show thee;

text.NRSV: I will make of you a great nation, and I will bless you, and make your name great, so that you will be a blessing.
text.Rotherham: That I may make thee into a great nation, And bless thee and make great thy name, And become thou a blessing;

text.NRSV: I will bless those who bless you, and the one who curses you I will curse; and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed.”
text.Rotherham: That I may bless them who bless thee, But him who maketh light of thee, will I curse,—So shall be, blessed in thee, all the families of the ground.

text.NRSV: So Abram went, as the Lord had told him; and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
text.Rotherham: And Abram came on his way, according to that which Yahweh had spoken unto him, and Lot came with him,—now, Abram, was seventy-five years old, when he came forth out of Haran.

text.NRSV: Abram took his wife Sarai and his brother’s son Lot, and all the possessions that they had gathered, and the persons whom they had acquired in Haran; and they set forth to go to the land of Canaan. When they had come to the land of Canaan,
text.Rotherham: And Abram took Sarai his wife, and Lot his brother’s son, with all the goods that they had gathered and the souls that they had gained in Haran,—and they came forth to go towards the land of Canaan, and came in to the land of Canaan.
