text.NRSV: Abram passed through the land to the place at Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.
text.Rotherham: And Abram passed along throughout the land, as far as the place of Shechem as far as the Teacher’s Terebinth, the Canaanite being then in the land.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord appeared to Abram, and said, “To your offspring I will give this land.” So he built there an altar to the Lord, who had appeared to him.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared unto Abram, and said: To thy seed, will I give this land,—And he built there an altar, unto Yahweh who appeared unto him.

text.NRSV: From there he moved on to the hill country on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east; and there he built an altar to the Lord and invoked the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he moved on from thence towards the hill country, on the east of Bethel and pitched his tent,—with Bethel on the west, and Ai on the east, and built there an altar to Yahweh, and called on the name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: And Abram journeyed on by stages toward the Negeb.
text.Rotherham: Thus Abram brake up, again and again, towards the South.

text.NRSV: Now there was a famine in the land. So Abram went down to Egypt to reside there as an alien, for the famine was severe in the land.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that there was a famine, in the land,—so Abram went down towards Egypt, to sojourn there, because grievous, was the famine in the land.

text.NRSV: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to his wife Sarai, “I know well that you are a woman beautiful in appearance;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when he had come near to enter into Egypt, that he said unto Sarai his wife: Behold! I beseech thee I know that a woman fair to look on, thou art:

text.NRSV: and when the Egyptians see you, they will say, ‘This is his wife’; then they will kill me, but they will let you live.
text.Rotherham: so will it come to pass when the Egyptians behold thee, that they will say, His wife, this! and will slay me while thee, they preserve alive.

text.NRSV: Say you are my sister, so that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared on your account.”
text.Rotherham: Say, I beseech thee that my sister, art thou,—to the end it may be well with me for thy sake, so shall my soul be preserved alive because of thee.

text.NRSV: When Abram entered Egypt the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
text.Rotherham: And so it was when Abram entered into Egypt, the Egyptians beheld the woman, that fair, was she exceedingly.

text.NRSV: When the officials of Pharaoh saw her, they praised her to Pharaoh. And the woman was taken into Pharaoh’s house.
text.Rotherham: And the princes of Pharaoh beheld her, and praised her unto Pharaoh,—so the woman was taken to the house of Pharaoh;

text.NRSV: And for her sake he dealt well with Abram; and he had sheep, oxen, male donkeys, male and female slaves, female donkeys, and camels.
text.Rotherham: and with Abram, dealt he well for her sake,—so that he came to have flocks and herds and he-asses, and men-servants, and maid-servants, and she-asses and camels.

text.NRSV: But the Lord afflicted Pharaoh and his house with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram’s wife.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh plagued Pharaoh with great plagues, also his house,—for the matter of Sarai, wife of Abram.

text.NRSV: So Pharaoh called Abram, and said, “What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh called out to Abram, and said, What is this thou hast done to me? Wherefore didst thou not tell me, that she was, thy wife?

text.NRSV: Why did you say, ‘She is my sister,’ so that I took her for my wife? Now then, here is your wife, take her, and be gone.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore saidst thou My sister, she; and so I was about to take her to me, to wife? But now, lo! thy wife take her and go thy way.

text.NRSV: And Pharaoh gave his men orders concerning him; and they set him on the way, with his wife and all that he had.
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh gave command concerning him unto certain men,—and sent him away, with his wife and all that he had.

text.NRSV: So Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife, and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negeb.
text.Rotherham: So Abram came up out of Egypt he and his wife and all that he had and Lot with him, towards the South.

text.NRSV: Now Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.
text.Rotherham: Now, Abram, was very rich,—in cattle, in silver and in gold.

text.NRSV: He journeyed on by stages from the Negeb as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,
text.Rotherham: And he went his way, by his removals, from the South even as far as to Bethel,—as far as the place where his tent was at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai:

text.NRSV: to the place where he had made an altar at the first; and there Abram called on the name of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: unto the place of the altar, which he made there at first,—and Abram called there, on the name of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Now Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents,
text.Rotherham: Now, Lot also, who was going with Abram, had flocks and herds and tents.

text.NRSV: so that the land could not support both of them living together; for their possessions were so great that they could not live together,
text.Rotherham: And the land suffered them not to dwell together,—because their substance had become, great, so that they could not dwell together.

text.NRSV: and there was strife between the herders of Abram’s livestock and the herders of Lot’s livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites lived in the land.
text.Rotherham: And there arose a strife betwixt the herdmen of Abram, and the herdmen of Lot. Now the Canaanite and the Perizzite, were then dwelling in the land.

text.NRSV: Then Abram said to Lot, “Let there be no strife between you and me, and between your herders and my herders; for we are kindred.
text.Rotherham: So then Abram said unto Lot Pray let not cause of strife arise betwixt me and thee, or betwixt my herdmen and thy herdmen; for brethren, are we.

text.NRSV: Is not the whole land before you? Separate yourself from me. If you take the left hand, then I will go to the right; or if you take the right hand, then I will go to the left.”
text.Rotherham: Is not, all the land, before thee? I pray thee, separate thyself from me,—if to the left hand, then I will go to the right, if to the right hand, then I will go to the left.

text.NRSV: Lot looked about him, and saw that the plain of the Jordan was well watered everywhere like the garden of the Lord, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Zoar; this was before the Lord had destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.
text.Rotherham: So Lot lifted up his eyes and beheld all the circuit of the Jordan, that the whole of it, was well-watered,—before Yahweh destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah, like the Garden of Yahweh, like the land of Egypt, as thou enterest into Zoar.

text.NRSV: So Lot chose for himself all the plain of the Jordan, and Lot journeyed eastward; thus they separated from each other.
text.Rotherham: And Lot chose for himself all the circuit of the Jordan, so Lot brake up eastwards,—and they separated themselves, each man from his brother:

text.NRSV: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled among the cities of the Plain and moved his tent as far as Sodom.
text.Rotherham: Abram, fixed his dwelling in the land of Canaan,—but, Lot, fixed his dwelling among the cities, of the circuit, and moved his tent as far as Sodom.

text.NRSV: Now the people of Sodom were wicked, great sinners against the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men of Sodom were base and sinful,—against Yahweh, exceedingly.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, “Raise your eyes now, and look from the place where you are, northward and southward and eastward and westward;
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, said unto Abram after that Lot had separated himself from him, Lift up, I pray thee thine eyes and look, from the place where thou art,—northward and southward and eastward and westward;

text.NRSV: for all the land that you see I will give to you and to your offspring forever.
text.Rotherham: for all the land which thou art beholding—to thee, will I give it, and to thy seed unto times age-abiding;

text.NRSV: I will make your offspring like the dust of the earth; so that if one can count the dust of the earth, your offspring also can be counted.
text.Rotherham: and I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth,—so that if a man can number the dust of the earth, thy seed also, may be numbered.

text.NRSV: Rise up, walk through the length and the breadth of the land, for I will give it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Rise! go up and down in the land, to the length thereof and to the breadth thereof, for to thee, will I give it.

text.NRSV: So Abram moved his tent, and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are at Hebron; and there he built an altar to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So Abram moved his tent and came in and dwelt among the oaks of Mamre, which were in Hebron,—and built there an altar to Yahweh.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Amraphel of Shinar, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the days of Amraphel, king of Shinar, Arioch, king of Ellasar,—Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goim,

text.NRSV: these kings made war with King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
text.Rotherham: that they made war, with Bera, king of Sodom, and with Birsha, king of Gomorrah,—Shinab, king of Admah, and Shemeber, king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela—the same, is Zoar.

text.NRSV: All these joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Dead Sea).
text.Rotherham: All these, joined together in the valley of the open fields, the same, is the Salt Sea.

text.NRSV: Twelve years they had served Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled.
text.Rotherham: Twelve years, had they served Chedorlaomer,—but in the thirteenth year had they rebelled;

text.NRSV: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and subdued the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
text.Rotherham: and in the fourteenth year, had Chedorlaomer come in and the kings who were with him, so they smote the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, and the Zuzim in Ham,—and the Emim, in Shaveh-kiriathaim;

text.NRSV: and the Horites in the hill country of Seir as far as El-paran on the edge of the wilderness;
text.Rotherham: and the Horites in their Mount Seir,—as far as El-paran, which is by the desert.

text.NRSV: then they turned back and came to En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and subdued all the country of the Amalekites, and also the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
text.Rotherham: So they returned and came in unto En-mishpat, the same, is Kadish, and smote all the field of the Amalekites,—and the Amorites also that dwelt in Hazazon-tamar,

text.NRSV: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out, and they joined battle in the Valley of Siddim
text.Rotherham: Thus then went forth the King of Sodom and the king of Gomorrah, and the king of Admah and the king of Zeboim, and the king of Bela the same, is Zoar—and set themselves in array against them for battle, in the valley of the open fields:

text.NRSV: with King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar, four kings against five.
text.Rotherham: against Chedorlaomer king of Elam, and Tidal king of Goim, and Amraphel king of Shinar, and Arioch king of Ellasar,—four kings against five.

text.NRSV: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of bitumen pits; and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, and the rest fled to the hill country.
text.Rotherham: Now, the valley of the open fields, had many pits of bitumen, so the king of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, and fell there,—while, they who remained, towards a mountain, fled.

text.NRSV: So the enemy took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah, and all their provisions, and went their way;
text.Rotherham: And they took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food and went their way.

text.NRSV: they also took Lot, the son of Abram’s brother, who lived in Sodom, and his goods, and departed.
text.Rotherham: And they took Lot, Abram’s brother’s son with his goods, and went their way,—he, being a dweller in Sodom.

text.NRSV: Then one who had escaped came and told Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oaks of Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and of Aner; these were allies of Abram.
text.Rotherham: Then came in a fugitive, and told Abram the Hebrew,—he, having his dwelling among the oaks of Mamre the Amorite brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner, they, also having a covenant with Abram.

text.NRSV: When Abram heard that his nephew had been taken captive, he led forth his trained men, born in his house, three hundred eighteen of them, and went in pursuit as far as Dan.
text.Rotherham: So Abram, hearing that his brother had been taken captive, drew forth his trained men born in his house three hundred and eighteen, and pursued as far as Dan.

text.NRSV: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and routed them and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus.
text.Rotherham: And he divided himself against them by night, he, and his servants, and smote them,—and pursued them as far as Hobah, which was on the left of Damascus.

text.NRSV: Then he brought back all the goods, and also brought back his nephew Lot with his goods, and the women and the people.
text.Rotherham: So he brought back all the goods,—yea Lot also his brother with his goods, did he bring back, and the women also, and the people.

text.NRSV: After his return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom went out to meet him at the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley).
text.Rotherham: Then came forth the king of Sodom to meet him, after his return from the smiting of Chedorlaomer, and the kings who were with him,—into the vale of Shaveh the same, was the vale of the king.

text.NRSV: And King Melchizedek of Salem brought out bread and wine; he was priest of God Most High.
text.Rotherham: Now Melchizedek king of Salem, had brought forth bread and wine,—he, being priest of GOD Most High.

text.NRSV: He blessed him and said, “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, maker of heaven and earth;
text.Rotherham: So he blessed him and said,—Blessed be Abram of GOD Most High, possessor of [the] heavens and earth;

text.NRSV: and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand!” And Abram gave him one tenth of everything.
text.Rotherham: And blessed be GOD Most High, who hath delivered thine enemies into thy hand. So he gave unto him a tenth of all.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the persons, but take the goods for yourself.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Sodom unto Abram,—Give unto me the persons, but the goods, take thou for thyself.

text.NRSV: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, “I have sworn to the Lord, God Most High, maker of heaven and earth,
text.Rotherham: And Abram said unto the king of Sodom,—I have lifted up my hand unto Yahweh GOD Most High, possessor of [the] heavens and earth:

text.NRSV: that I would not take a thread or a sandal-thong or anything that is yours, so that you might not say, ‘I have made Abram rich.’
text.Rotherham: That not from a thread even unto a sandal-thong,—will I take, anything, that is thine,—Lest thou shouldst say, I, enriched Abram!

text.NRSV: I will take nothing but what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre. Let them take their share.”
text.Rotherham: Save only what the young men have eaten, and the share of the men who went with me, Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre, they may take their share.

text.NRSV: After these things the word of the Lord came to Abram in a vision, “Do not be afraid, Abram, I am your shield; your reward shall be very great.”
text.Rotherham: After these things, came the word of Yahweh unto Abram, in a vision saying,—Do not fear Abram, I am a shield to thee, thine exceeding great reward.

text.NRSV: But Abram said, “O Lord God, what will you give me, for I continue childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?”
text.Rotherham: And Abram said My Lord Yahweh, what canst thou give me, when, I, am going on childless,—and the heir of my house, is Eliezer, of Damascus,

text.NRSV: And Abram said, “You have given me no offspring, and so a slave born in my house is to be my heir.”
text.Rotherham: And Abram said—Lo, to me, hast thou not given seed,—And lo! a son of my household, is mine heir!

text.NRSV: But the word of the Lord came to him, “This man shall not be your heir; no one but your very own issue shall be your heir.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! the word of Yahweh [came] unto him, saying, This one, shall not be thine heir: But one who cometh forth of thy body—he shall be thine heir.

text.NRSV: He brought him outside and said, “Look toward heaven and count the stars, if you are able to count them.” Then he said to him, “So shall your descendants be.”
text.Rotherham: And he brought him forth abroad and said—Look steadfastly. I pray thee towards the heavens, and number the stars, if thou be able to number, them, And he said to him, Thus, shall be thy seed.

text.NRSV: And he believed the Lord; and the Lord reckoned it to him as righteousness.
text.Rotherham: And he had faith in Yahweh,—so he reckoned it to him as righteousness.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “I am the Lord who brought you from Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to possess.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him,—I, am Yahweh, who brought thee forth out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give thee this land to inherit it.

text.NRSV: But he said, “O Lord God, how am I to know that I shall possess it?”
text.Rotherham: And he said, My Lord Yahweh, whereby, can I know that I shall inherit it?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Bring me a heifer three years old, a female goat three years old, a ram three years old, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, Take for me a heifer three years old, and a she-goat three years old, and a ram three years old, and a turtle-dove and a young pigeon.

text.NRSV: He brought him all these and cut them in two, laying each half over against the other; but he did not cut the birds in two.
text.Rotherham: So he took for him all these, and divided them, in the midst, and placed each piece over against its fellow, but the birds, divided he not.

text.NRSV: And when birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
text.Rotherham: Then came down the birds of prey upon the carcass, and Abram drove them away.

text.NRSV: As the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram, and a deep and terrifying darkness descended upon him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when, the sun, was about to go in, and, a deep sleep, had fallen upon Abram, lo! a terror a great darkness, was falling upon him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Abram, “Know this for certain, that your offspring shall be aliens in a land that is not theirs, and shall be slaves there, and they shall be oppressed for four hundred years;
text.Rotherham: And he said to Abram—Thou must surely know, that, sojourners, will thy seed become in a land not theirs, and shall serve them and they will humble them, four hundred years;

text.NRSV: but I will bring judgment on the nation that they serve, and afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
text.Rotherham: moreover also, the nation whom they will serve, I, am going to judge, and after that, shall they come forth with great substance.

text.NRSV: As for yourself, you shall go to your ancestors in peace; you shall be buried in a good old age.
text.Rotherham: But, thou, shalt go in unto thy fathers in peace, thou shalt be buried in a good old age.

text.NRSV: And they shall come back here in the fourth generation; for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.”
text.Rotherham: And, in the fourth generation, shall they return hither,—for not complete, is the iniquity of the Amorites, as yet,

text.NRSV: When the sun had gone down and it was dark, a smoking fire pot and a flaming torch passed between these pieces.
text.Rotherham: So it came to pass when, the sun, had gone in, and, a thick darkness, had come on, that lo there was a smoking hearth and torch of fire, which passed through between these pieces.

text.NRSV: On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram, saying, “To your descendants I give this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the river Euphrates,
text.Rotherham: In that day, did Yahweh solemnise with Abram a covenant, saying,—To thy seed, have I given this land, from the river of Egypt as far as the great river—the river Euphrates:

text.NRSV: the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text.Rotherham: the Kenite, and the Kenizzite, and the Kadmonite;

text.NRSV: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
text.Rotherham: and the Hittite and the Perizzite, and the Rephaim;

text.NRSV: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.”
text.Rotherham: and the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Girgashite, and the Jebusite.

text.NRSV: Now Sarai, Abram’s wife, bore him no children. She had an Egyptian slave-girl whose name was Hagar,
text.Rotherham: But Sarai, Abram’s wife, had borne him no children,—she had, however, an Egyptian handmaid, whose name was Hagar.

text.NRSV: and Sarai said to Abram, “You see that the Lord has prevented me from bearing children; go in to my slave-girl; it may be that I shall obtain children by her.” And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.
text.Rotherham: So then Sarai said unto Abram Behold, I pray thee, Yahweh hath restrained me, from bearing, go in I pray thee unto, my handmaid, peradventure I may be built up from, her, And Abram hearkened to the voice of Sarai.

text.NRSV: So, after Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram’s wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her slave-girl, and gave her to her husband Abram as a wife.
text.Rotherham: So Sarai, Abram’s wife, took Hagar the Egyptian. her handmaid, at the end of ten years of Abram’s dwelling in the land of Canaan, and gave her to Abram her husband, to be to him as a wife.

text.NRSV: He went in to Hagar, and she conceived; and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked with contempt on her mistress.
text.Rotherham: And he went in unto Hagar and she conceived,—and when she saw that she had conceived, lightly esteemed, was her lady, in her eyes.

text.NRSV: Then Sarai said to Abram, “May the wrong done to me be on you! I gave my slave-girl to your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, she looked on me with contempt. May the Lord judge between you and me!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Sarai unto Abram: My wrong, is, on thee! I, gave my handmaid into thy bosom, and when she seeth that she hath conceived, then am I lightly esteemed in her eyes Yahweh judge betwixt me and her.

text.NRSV: But Abram said to Sarai, “Your slave-girl is in your power; do to her as you please.” Then Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she ran away from her.
text.Rotherham: And Abram said unto Sarai, Lo! thy handmaid, is in thy hand, do to her what is good in thine eyes. So Sarai humbled her, and she fled from her face.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, the spring on the way to Shur.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh found her by the fountain of water in the desert,—by the fountain in the way to Shur.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Hagar, slave-girl of Sarai, where have you come from and where are you going?” She said, “I am running away from my mistress Sarai.”
text.Rotherham: So he said Hagar! handmaid of Sarai! Whence hast thou come, and whither wouldst thou go? And she said, From the face of Sarai, my lady, am, I, fleeing.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord said to her, “Return to your mistress, and submit to her.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, Return unto thy lady,—and humble thyself under her hands.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord also said to her, “I will so greatly multiply your offspring that they cannot be counted for multitude.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, I will, greatly multiply, thy seed—so that it shall not be numbered for multitude.

text.NRSV: And the angel of the Lord said to her, “Now you have conceived and shall bear a son; you shall call him Ishmael, for the Lord has given heed to your affliction.
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh said to her, Behold thee! with child, and about bearing a son—and thou shalt call his name Ishmael [="God hearkeneth"] for Yahweh hath hearkened unto thy humiliation.

text.NRSV: He shall be a wild ass of a man, with his hand against everyone, and everyone’s hand against him; and he shall live at odds with all his kin.”
text.Rotherham: But, he, will be a wild ass of a man, his hand, against every one, and every one’s hand against him—yet, in presence of all his brethren, shall he have his habitation.

text.NRSV: So she named the Lord who spoke to her, “You are El-roi”; for she said, “Have I really seen God and remained alive after seeing him?”
text.Rotherham: And she called the name of Yahweh, who had spoken unto her, Thou GOD of vision! For she said. Do I even here, retain my vision after a vision?

text.NRSV: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; it lies between Kadesh and Bered.
text.Rotherham: On this account, is the well called, the well of the life of vision, lo! it is between Kadesh and Bered.

text.NRSV: Hagar bore Abram a son; and Abram named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: So Hagar bare to Abram a son,—and Abram called the name of his son, whom Hagar bare, Ishmael.

text.NRSV: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore him Ishmael.
text.Rotherham: Now, Abram, was eighty—six years old,—when Hagar bare Ishmael to Abram.

text.NRSV: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the Lord appeared to Abram, and said to him, “I am God Almighty; walk before me, and be blameless.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, when Abram was ninety and nine years old, Yahweh appeared unto Abram, and said unto him, I, am GOD Almighty,—Walk, thou before me and become thou blameless:

text.NRSV: And I will make my covenant between me and you, and will make you exceedingly numerous.”
text.Rotherham: That I may set my covenant betwixt me and thee, And may multiply thee, exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Then Abram fell on his face; and God said to him,
text.Rotherham: And Abram fell on his face,—and God spake with him, saying:

text.NRSV: “As for me, this is my covenant with you: You shall be the ancestor of a multitude of nations.
text.Rotherham: As for me, lo! my covenant is with thee,—So shalt thou become—father of a multitude of nations;

text.NRSV: No longer shall your name be Abram, but your name shall be Abraham; for I have made you the ancestor of a multitude of nations.
text.Rotherham: And thy name shall no more be called Abram,—but thy name shall become Abraham, for father of a multitude of nations, have I appointed thee;

text.NRSV: I will make you exceedingly fruitful; and I will make nations of you, and kings shall come from you.
text.Rotherham: And I will make thee fruitful, exceedingly, and grant thee to be nations,—Yea kings, out of thee, shall come forth;

text.NRSV: I will establish my covenant between me and you, and your offspring after you throughout their generations, for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your offspring after you.
text.Rotherham: And I will confirm my covenant betwixt me and thee and thy seed after thee to their generations for an age-abiding covenant,—to become to thee a God, and to thy seed after thee;

text.NRSV: And I will give to you, and to your offspring after you, the land where you are now an alien, all the land of Canaan, for a perpetual holding; and I will be their God.”
text.Rotherham: And I will give to thee and to thy seed after thee the land of thy sojournings—all the land of Canaan, for an age-abiding possession—And I will be to them a God.

text.NRSV: God said to Abraham, “As for you, you shall keep my covenant, you and your offspring after you throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham, But, as for thee, my covenant, must thou keep, thou and thy seed after thee, to their generations:—

text.NRSV: This is my covenant, which you shall keep, between me and you and your offspring after you: Every male among you shall be circumcised.
text.Rotherham: This, is my covenant which ye shall keep, betwixt me and you, and thy seed after thee,—To circumcise to you every male:

text.NRSV: You shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between me and you.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskin,—So shall it become a sign of a covenant, betwixt me and you.

text.NRSV: Throughout your generations every male among you shall be circumcised when he is eight days old, including the slave born in your house and the one bought with your money from any foreigner who is not of your offspring.
text.Rotherham: And he that is eight days old, shall be circumcised to you every male to your generations,—he that is born of the house, and he that is bought with silver of any son of a stranger, who is, not of thy seed,

text.NRSV: Both the slave born in your house and the one bought with your money must be circumcised. So shall my covenant be in your flesh an everlasting covenant.
text.Rotherham: He must surely be circumcised, born of thy house or bought with thy silver,—So shall my covenant be in your flesh for an age-abiding covenant.

text.NRSV: Any uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin shall be cut off from his people; he has broken my covenant.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for the uncircumcised male who shall not be circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin,—that person shall be cut off from among his people,—my covenant, hath he made void.

text.NRSV: God said to Abraham, “As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai,—but, Sarah, is her name;

text.NRSV: I will bless her, and moreover I will give you a son by her. I will bless her, and she shall give rise to nations; kings of peoples shall come from her.”
text.Rotherham: and I will bless her, yea moreover will give—from her—to thee, a son,—And I will bless her, and she shall become nations. Kings of peoples, from her, shall arise.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said to himself, “Can a child be born to a man who is a hundred years old? Can Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham fell on his face and laughed,—and said in his heart To one a hundred years old, shall a child be born? And shall even Sarah, who is ninety years old, give birth?

text.NRSV: And Abraham said to God, “O that Ishmael might live in your sight!”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said unto God,—Oh that, Ishmael, might live before thee!

text.NRSV: God said, “No, but your wife Sarah shall bear you a son, and you shall name him Isaac. I will establish my covenant with him as an everlasting covenant for his offspring after him.
text.Rotherham: And God said—Truly, Sarah thy wife, is about to bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name, Isaac,—and I will establish my covenant with him as an age-abiding covenant, to his seed after him.

text.NRSV: As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him and make him fruitful and exceedingly numerous; he shall be the father of twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation.
text.Rotherham: Yet as for Ishmael, I have heard thee; lo! I have blessed him and will make him fruitful, and multiply him, exceedingly,—twelve princes, shall he beget, and I will grant him to be a great nation;

text.NRSV: But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this season next year.”
text.Rotherham: But my covenant, will I establish with Isaac,—whom Sarah shall bear, to thee, by this set time, in the next year.

text.NRSV: And when he had finished talking with him, God went up from Abraham.
text.Rotherham: And he left off speaking with him,—and God went up from Abraham.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham took his son Ishmael and all the slaves born in his house or bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham’s house, and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very day, as God had said to him.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham took Ishmael his son and all born of his house and all bought with his silver—every male among the men of the house of Abraham,—and circumcised the flesh of their foreskin on this selfsame day, according to that which God had spoken with him.

text.NRSV: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.Rotherham: Now Abraham, was ninety-nine years old,—when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

text.NRSV: And his son Ishmael was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.
text.Rotherham: And Ishmael, his son, was thirteen years old, when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin.

text.NRSV: That very day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised;
text.Rotherham: On this selfsame day, was Abraham circumcised,—and Ishmael his son;

text.NRSV: and all the men of his house, slaves born in the house and those bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him.
text.Rotherham: and, all the men of his house, born of his house, and bought with silver from the son of a stranger, were circumcised with him.

text.NRSV: The Lord appeared to Abraham by the oaks of Mamre, as he sat at the entrance of his tent in the heat of the day.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh appeared unto him among the oaks of Mamre,—as, he, was sitting at the opening of the tent in the heat of the day.

text.NRSV: He looked up and saw three men standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent entrance to meet them, and bowed down to the ground.
text.Rotherham: And he lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! three men, standing over against him,—so he looked and ran to meet them from the opening of the tent, and bowed himself to the earth;

text.NRSV: He said, “My lord, if I find favor with you, do not pass by your servant.
text.Rotherham: and said,—O My Lord! if, I pray thee, I have found favour in thine eyes, do not I pray thee pass on from thy servant.

text.NRSV: Let a little water be brought, and wash your feet, and rest yourselves under the tree.
text.Rotherham: Let there be fetched, I pray thee a little water, and bathe ye your feet,—and rest yourselves under the tree.

text.NRSV: Let me bring a little bread, that you may refresh yourselves, and after that you may pass on—since you have come to your servant.” So they said, “Do as you have said.”
text.Rotherham: And let me fetch a morsel of bread, and stay ye your heart, Afterwards, ye may pass on, For on this account, have ye passed by, over against your servant. And they said, Thus, shalt thou do as thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: And Abraham hastened into the tent to Sarah, and said, “Make ready quickly three measures of choice flour, knead it, and make cakes.”
text.Rotherham: So Abraham hastened towards the tent unto Sarah,—and said, Hasten thou three measures of fine meal, knead it, and make hearth-cakes.

text.NRSV: Abraham ran to the herd, and took a calf, tender and good, and gave it to the servant, who hastened to prepare it.
text.Rotherham: And unto the herd, ran Abraham,—and took a calf tender and good and gave unto the young man, and he hastened to make it ready.

text.NRSV: Then he took curds and milk and the calf that he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree while they ate.
text.Rotherham: And he took butter and milk and the calf that he had made ready, and set before them,—while, he, was standing near them under the tree, they did eat.

text.NRSV: They said to him, “Where is your wife Sarah?” And he said, “There, in the tent.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto him, As to Sarah thy wife… And he said Lo! [she is] in the tent.

text.NRSV: Then one said, “I will surely return to you in due season, and your wife Sarah shall have a son.” And Sarah was listening at the tent entrance behind him.
text.Rotherham: And he said, I will, surely return, unto thee at the quickening season,—and lo! a son for Sarah thy wife. Now Sarah, was hearkening at the opening of the tent, it, being behind him.

text.NRSV: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, advanced in age; it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.
text.Rotherham: But Abraham and Sarah, were old, far gone in days,—it had ceased to be with Sarah after the manner of women.

text.NRSV: So Sarah laughed to herself, saying, “After I have grown old, and my husband is old, shall I have pleasure?”
text.Rotherham: So then Sarah laughed within herself, saying: After I am past age, hath there come to me pleasure, my lord, also being old?

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh, and say, ‘Shall I indeed bear a child, now that I am old?’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Abraham,—Wherefore now did Sarah laugh saying. Can it really and truly be that I should bear, seeing that I have become old?

text.NRSV: Is anything too wonderful for the Lord? At the set time I will return to you, in due season, and Sarah shall have a son.”
text.Rotherham: Is anything, too wonderful for Yahweh? At the appointed time, I will return unto thee, at the quickening season and Sarah, shall have a son.

text.NRSV: But Sarah denied, saying, “I did not laugh”; for she was afraid. He said, “Oh yes, you did laugh.”
text.Rotherham: And Sarah denied, saying, I laughed not, For she was afraid. And he said—Nay! but thou didst laugh!

text.NRSV: Then the men set out from there, and they looked toward Sodom; and Abraham went with them to set them on their way.
text.Rotherham: And the men rose up from thence, and looked out over the face of Sodom,—Abraham also going with them to set them on their way.

text.NRSV: The Lord said, “Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, had said,—Am I going to hide from Abraham what I do,

text.NRSV: seeing that Abraham shall become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed in him?
text.Rotherham: when, Abraham, shall surely become, a great and mighty nation,—and all the nations of the earth, shall be blessed in him?

text.NRSV: No, for I have chosen him, that he may charge his children and his household after him to keep the way of the Lord by doing righteousness and justice; so that the Lord may bring about for Abraham what he has promised him.”
text.Rotherham: For I have become his intimate friend, To the end that he may command his sons and his house after him, so shall they keep the way of Yahweh, by doing righteousness and justice. To the end that Yahweh may bring in for Abraham, what he hath spoken concerning him.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “How great is the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah and how very grave their sin!
text.Rotherham: So Yahweh said, The outcry of Sodom and Gomorrah, because it hath become great,—And their sin, because it hath become exceeding grievous,

text.NRSV: I must go down and see whether they have done altogether according to the outcry that has come to me; and if not, I will know.”
text.Rotherham: Let me go down, pray, and let me behold, whether according to their outcry which hath come in unto me, they have done altogether, And if not. I must know!

text.NRSV: So the men turned from there, and went toward Sodom, while Abraham remained standing before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the men turned from thence, and went their way, towards Sodom,—but, Yahweh was yet standing before Abraham.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham came near and said, “Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
text.Rotherham: So then Abraham drew near and said, Wilt thou really sweep away, the righteous with the lawless?

text.NRSV: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; will you then sweep away the place and not forgive it for the fifty righteous who are in it?
text.Rotherham: Peradventure there are fifty righteous, in the midst of the city, Wilt thou really sweep away, and not spare the place, for the sake of the fifty righteous which are therein?

text.NRSV: Far be it from you to do such a thing, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous fare as the wicked! Far be that from you! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do what is just?”
text.Rotherham: Far be it from thee! to do after this manner. to put to death the righteous with the lawless! Then should righteous and lawless be alike, Far be it from thee! Shall, the Judge of all the earth not do justice?

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, “If I find at Sodom fifty righteous in the city, I will forgive the whole place for their sake.”
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, If I find in Sodom fifty righteous in the midst of the city, then will I spare all the place for their sake.

text.NRSV: Abraham answered, “Let me take it upon myself to speak to the Lord, I who am but dust and ashes.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham responded and said,—Behold I pray thee I have ventured to speak unto My Lord, though I am dust and ashes:

text.NRSV: Suppose five of the fifty righteous are lacking? Will you destroy the whole city for lack of five?” And he said, “I will not destroy it if I find forty-five there.”
text.Rotherham: Peradventure there may lack, of the fifty righteous, five, Wilt thou destroy for five all the city? And he said, I will not destroy, if I find there forty and five.

text.NRSV: Again he spoke to him, “Suppose forty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of forty I will not do it.”
text.Rotherham: And he added, yet once more, to speak unto him, and said, Peradventure there may be found there—forty. And he said—I will not do it, for the sake of the forty.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Oh do not let the Lord be angry if I speak. Suppose thirty are found there.” He answered, “I will not do it, if I find thirty there.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Let it not, I pray thee, be vexing to My Lord, but let me speak, Peradventure there may be found there—thirty And he said, I will not to it, if I find there—thirty.

text.NRSV: He said, “Let me take it upon myself to speak to the Lord. Suppose twenty are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of twenty I will not destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Behold, I pray thee, I have ventured to speak unto My Lord, Peradventure there may be found there—twenty. And he said I will not destroy it, for the sake of the twenty.

text.NRSV: Then he said, “Oh do not let the Lord be angry if I speak just once more. Suppose ten are found there.” He answered, “For the sake of ten I will not destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, Let it not, I pray thee, be vexing to My Lord, but let me speak only this once, Peradventure there may be found there—ten. And he said, I will not destroy [it], for the sake of the ten!

text.NRSV: And the Lord went his way, when he had finished speaking to Abraham; and Abraham returned to his place.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh went his way, when he had made an end. of speaking unto Abraham,—Abraham, also returned to his place.

text.NRSV: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gateway of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them, and bowed down with his face to the ground.
text.Rotherham: So the two messengers went in towards Sodom at eventide, Lot, being seated in the gate of Sodom,—so Lot beheld and rose up to meet them, and bowed himself with his face to the earth.

text.NRSV: He said, “Please, my lords, turn aside to your servant’s house and spend the night, and wash your feet; then you can rise early and go on your way.” They said, “No; we will spend the night in the square.”
text.Rotherham: And he said Behold, I pray you my lords turn aside, I pray you, into the house of your servant,—and tarry the night and bathe your feet, so shall ye rise early, and go on your way. And they said, Nay, but, in the broad way, will we tarry the night.

text.NRSV: But he urged them strongly; so they turned aside to him and entered his house; and he made them a feast, and baked unleavened bread, and they ate.
text.Rotherham: But he became exceeding urgent with them, so they turned aside unto him and entered into his house,—and he made for them a banquet, unleavened cakes, also did he bake, and they did eat.

text.NRSV: But before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people to the last man, surrounded the house;
text.Rotherham: Ere yet they lay down, the men of the city—the men of Sodom, had come round against the house, from youth even unto age,—all the people from every quarter,

text.NRSV: and they called to Lot, “Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us, so that we may know them.”
text.Rotherham: And they called unto Lot and said to him, Where are the men who have come in unto thee tonight? Bring them forth unto us, and let us know them!

text.NRSV: Lot went out of the door to the men, shut the door after him,
text.Rotherham: And Lot went forth unto them to the entrance; but the door, closed he behind him.

text.NRSV: and said, “I beg you, my brothers, do not act so wickedly.
text.Rotherham: And he said,—Do not I pray you my brethren act wickedly.

text.NRSV: Look, I have two daughters who have not known a man; let me bring them out to you, and do to them as you please; only do nothing to these men, for they have come under the shelter of my roof.”
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray you, I, have two daughters who have not known man, I must needs now bring, them, forth unto you, and do ye to them, as may be good in your eyes,—Only to these men, ye may do nothing; for on this account, have they come under the shade of my roof.

text.NRSV: But they replied, “Stand back!” And they said, “This fellow came here as an alien, and he would play the judge! Now we will deal worse with you than with them.” Then they pressed hard against the man Lot, and came near the door to break it down.
text.Rotherham: And they said, Stand back, And they said, This one, by himself, hath come in to sojourn and must always be acting the judge, Now, will we do more wickedly to thee, than to them. So they pressed sore upon the man—upon Lot—exceedingly, and drew near to break open the door.

text.NRSV: But the men inside reached out their hands and brought Lot into the house with them, and shut the door.
text.Rotherham: And the men thrust forth their hand, and brought Lot in unto them into the house—and, the door, they closed:

text.NRSV: And they struck with blindness the men who were at the door of the house, both small and great, so that they were unable to find the door.
text.Rotherham: the men also who were at the entrance of the house, smote they with blindness, from small even unto great, so that they wearied themselves to find the entrance.

text.NRSV: Then the men said to Lot, “Have you anyone else here? Sons-in-law, sons, daughters, or anyone you have in the city—bring them out of the place.
text.Rotherham: And the men said unto Lot, Whom besides hast thou here? Son-in-law and thy sons and thy daughters, and all that thou hast in the city, bring thou forth out of the place;

text.NRSV: For we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against its people has become great before the Lord, and the Lord has sent us to destroy it.”
text.Rotherham: For we are about to destroy this place,—for, great, is the outcry of them unto the face of Yahweh, so that Yahweh hath sent us to destroy it.

text.NRSV: So Lot went out and said to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, “Up, get out of this place; for the Lord is about to destroy the city.” But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be jesting.
text.Rotherham: And Lot went forth and spake unto his sons-in-law who were about to take his two daughters and said Arise come forth out of this place, for Yahweh is about to destroy the city. And it came to pass, that he was as one that laughed, in the eyes of his sons-in-law.

text.NRSV: When morning dawned, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Get up, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or else you will be consumed in the punishment of the city.”
text.Rotherham: But as soon as, the dawn had sprung up, the messengers hastened Lot saying,—Arise! take thy wife and thy two daughters that are at hand, lest thou be swept away in the punishment of the city.

text.NRSV: But he lingered; so the men seized him and his wife and his two daughters by the hand, the Lord being merciful to him, and they brought him out and left him outside the city.
text.Rotherham: And he lingered—so the men laid hold of his hand and of the hand of his wife and of the hand of his two daughters, in the tenderness of Yahweh towards him,—and they brought him out and led him forth outside the city.

text.NRSV: When they had brought them outside, they said, “Flee for your life; do not look back or stop anywhere in the Plain; flee to the hills, or else you will be consumed.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when they had brought them forth outside, that they said, Escape for thy life, Look not behind thee, neither stand still in all the vale,—To the mountain, escape thou lest thou be swept away.

text.NRSV: And Lot said to them, “Oh, no, my lords;
text.Rotherham: And Lot said unto them,—No, I pray thee, My Lord!

text.NRSV: your servant has found favor with you, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot flee to the hills, for fear the disaster will overtake me and I die.
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray thee thy servant hath found favour in thine eyes, so that thou hast magnified thy lovingkindness which thou hast performed with me in keeping alive my soul.—But, I, cannot escape to the mountain, lest calamity overtake me so shall I die.

text.NRSV: Look, that city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one. Let me escape there—is it not a little one?—and my life will be saved!”
text.Rotherham: Behold, I pray thee, this city, is near for fleeing thither And, it, is a little one, let me I pray thee, escape thither—is it not a, little, one? That my soul may live!

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Very well, I grant you this favor too, and will not overthrow the city of which you have spoken.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him, Behold! I have lifted up thy countenance, even as to this thing,—so that I will not overthrow the city, of which thou hast spoken.

text.NRSV: Hurry, escape there, for I can do nothing until you arrive there.” Therefore the city was called Zoar.
text.Rotherham: Haste thee escape thither, for I cannot do anything, until thou have come in thither. For this cause: was the name of the city called Zoar.

text.NRSV: The sun had risen on the earth when Lot came to Zoar.
text.Rotherham: When, the sun, had come forth on the earth, Lot had entered into Zoar.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord rained on Sodom and Gomorrah sulfur and fire from the Lord out of heaven;
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, rained, upon Sodom and upon Gomorrah, brimstone and fire,—from Yahweh, out of the heavens:

text.NRSV: and he overthrew those cities, and all the Plain, and all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
text.Rotherham: so he overthrew these cities, and all the circuit,—and all the inhabitants of the cities, and the produce of the ground.

text.NRSV: But Lot’s wife, behind him, looked back, and she became a pillar of salt.
text.Rotherham: But his wife looked from behind him,—and became—a pillar of salt.

text.NRSV: Abraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And Abraham gat up early in the morning, unto the place where he had stood before Yahweh;

text.NRSV: and he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and toward all the land of the Plain and saw the smoke of the land going up like the smoke of a furnace.
text.Rotherham: and he looked out over the face of Sodom and Gomorrah, and over all the face of the land of the circuit, and beheld and lo! the smoke of the land went up, like the smoke of a furnace.

text.NRSV: So it was that, when God destroyed the cities of the Plain, God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities in which Lot had settled.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass when God destroyed the cities of the circuit, that God remembered Abraham, and sent forth Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when he overthrew the cities wherein Lot had dwelt.

text.NRSV: Now Lot went up out of Zoar and settled in the hills with his two daughters, for he was afraid to stay in Zoar; so he lived in a cave with his two daughters.
text.Rotherham: And Lot went up out of Zoar, and dwelt in the mountain and his two daughters with him. for he feared to dwell in Zoar, so he dwelt in a cave, he, and his two daughters.

text.NRSV: And the firstborn said to the younger, “Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us after the manner of all the world.
text.Rotherham: And the firstborn said unto the younger. Our father is old,—and, a man, there is not in the earth to come in unto us, after the way of all the earth.

text.NRSV: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, so that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
text.Rotherham: Come on! let us cause our father to drink wine and let us lie with him, that we may keep alive from our father, a seed.

text.NRSV: So they made their father drink wine that night; and the firstborn went in, and lay with her father; he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.
text.Rotherham: So they caused their father to drink wine that night,—and the firstborn went in. and lay with her father, but he noticed not her lying down nor her rising up.

text.NRSV: On the next day, the firstborn said to the younger, “Look, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also; then you go in and lie with him, so that we may preserve offspring through our father.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the morrow, that the firstborn said unto the younger, Lo! I lay, last night with my father, let us cause him to drink wine this night also, and go thou in—lie with him, that we may keep alive from our father, a seed.

text.NRSV: So they made their father drink wine that night also; and the younger rose, and lay with him; and he did not know when she lay down or when she rose.
text.Rotherham: So they caused their father, on that night also to drink wine, and the younger arose, and lay with him, but he noticed not her lying down, nor her rising up.

text.NRSV: Thus both the daughters of Lot became pregnant by their father.
text.Rotherham: Thus did, the two daughters of Lot conceive from their father.

text.NRSV: The firstborn bore a son, and named him Moab; he is the ancestor of the Moabites to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the firstborn bare a son, and called his name Moab,—the same, is the father of Moab, unto this day.

text.NRSV: The younger also bore a son and named him Ben-ammi; he is the ancestor of the Ammonites to this day.
text.Rotherham: And the younger she also bare a son, and called his name Ben-ammi,—the same, is the father of the sons of Ammon—unto this day.

text.NRSV: From there Abraham journeyed toward the region of the Negeb, and settled between Kadesh and Shur. While residing in Gerar as an alien,
text.Rotherham: And Abraham brake up from thence, towards the land of the South, and fixed his dwelling between Kadesh and Shur, so he sojourned in Gerar.

text.NRSV: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, “She is my sister.” And King Abimelech of Gerar sent and took Sarah.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said of Sarah his wife: My, sister, is she,—So Abimelech king of Gerar sent, and took Sarah.

text.NRSV: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night, and said to him, “You are about to die because of the woman whom you have taken; for she is a married woman.”
text.Rotherham: Then God went in unto Abimelech, in a dream of the night,—and said to him, Behold thee dead! because of the woman whom thou hast taken, seeing that, she, is a married woman.

text.NRSV: Now Abimelech had not approached her; so he said, “Lord, will you destroy an innocent people?
text.Rotherham: Now, Abimelech, had not come near unto her,—so he said, O My Lord! a nation even a righteous one, wilt thou slay?

text.NRSV: Did he not himself say to me, ‘She is my sister’? And she herself said, ‘He is my brother.’ I did this in the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands.”
text.Rotherham: Had not, he himself, said to me, My sister, is she? and even she herself, said, My brother, is he? In the integrity of my heart and in the pureness of my hand, have I done this!

text.NRSV: Then God said to him in the dream, “Yes, I know that you did this in the integrity of your heart; furthermore it was I who kept you from sinning against me. Therefore I did not let you touch her.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto him in a dream, I, also, knew, that in the integrity of thy heart, thou didst this, so then, even I myself, withheld thee from sinning against me, for this reason, have I not suffered thee to touch her.

text.NRSV: Now then, return the man’s wife; for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you and you shall live. But if you do not restore her, know that you shall surely die, you and all that are yours.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore restore the man’s wife, for a prophet, is he, that he may pray for thee and live thou,—But if thou art not going to restore her, know, that thou shalt die, thou—and all that are thine.

text.NRSV: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants and told them all these things; and the men were very much afraid.
text.Rotherham: So Abimelech rose early in the morning, and called all his servants, and spake all these words in their ears, and the men feared greatly.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, “What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought such great guilt on me and my kingdom? You have done things to me that ought not to be done.”
text.Rotherham: Then Abimelech called Abraham, and said to him, What hast thou done to us? and wherein had I sinned against thee, that thou shouldst have brought in over me and over my kingdom, a sin so great? Deeds, which should not be done, hast thou done with me.

text.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What were you thinking of, that you did this thing?”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said unto Abraham,—What hadst thou seen, that thou shouldst have done this thing?

text.NRSV: Abraham said, “I did it because I thought, There is no fear of God at all in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, Because I thought, Surely there is no fear of God, in this place,—therefore will they slay me for the sake of my wife.

text.NRSV: Besides, she is indeed my sister, the daughter of my father but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, in truth, my sister daughter of my father, she is, only not daughter of my mother,—so she became my wife.

text.NRSV: And when God caused me to wander from my father’s house, I said to her, ‘This is the kindness you must do me: at every place to which we come, say of me, He is my brother.’”
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass when the Gods caused me to wander from my father’s house, that I said to her, This, is thy lovingkindness, wherewith thou shalt deal with me,—Into whatsoever place we enter, say of me My brother, is he.

text.NRSV: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen, and male and female slaves, and gave them to Abraham, and restored his wife Sarah to him.
text.Rotherham: Then took Abimelech sheep and oxen and men—servants and maid-servants, and gave to Abraham,—and restored to him Sarah his wife.

text.NRSV: Abimelech said, “My land is before you; settle where it pleases you.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech, said, Lo! my land is before thee,—wherever it may seem good in thine eyes, dwell.

text.NRSV: To Sarah he said, “Look, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; it is your exoneration before all who are with you; you are completely vindicated.”
text.Rotherham: And to Sarah, he said, Lo! I have given a thousand of silver unto thy brother: Lo! that is for thee as a covering of eyes, to all who are with thee,—And so in every way, hath right been done.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham prayed to God; and God healed Abimelech, and also healed his wife and female slaves so that they bore children.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham prayed unto God,—and God healed Abimelech, and his wife, and his maid-servants, so that they bare children,

text.NRSV: For the Lord had closed fast all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham’s wife.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh, had restrained from bearing, every female of the house of Abimelech,—because of Sarah, wife of Abraham.

text.NRSV: The Lord dealt with Sarah as he had said, and the Lord did for Sarah as he had promised.
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, visited Sarah, as he had said,—And Yahweh did for Sarah as he had spoken.

text.NRSV: Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the time of which God had spoken to him.
text.Rotherham: So, Sarah, conceived and bare, to Abraham, a son, for his old age, at the set time, of which God had spoken with him.

text.NRSV: Abraham gave the name Isaac to his son whom Sarah bore him.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham called the name of his son that was born to him, whom, Sarah, bare to him, Isaac.

text.NRSV: And Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham circumcised Isaac his son, when eight days old,—as God commanded him.

text.NRSV: Abraham was a hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
text.Rotherham: Now Abraham was a hundred years old,—when Isaac his son was born to him.

text.NRSV: Now Sarah said, “God has brought laughter for me; everyone who hears will laugh with me.”
text.Rotherham: And Sarah said, A laughing, hath God made for me,—Everyone that heareth will laugh with me.

text.NRSV: And she said, “Who would ever have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—Who would have announced to Abraham, Sarah hath suckled children? yet have I borne a son, for his old age!

text.NRSV: The child grew, and was weaned; and Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
text.Rotherham: And the child grew, and was weaned,—and Abraham made a great banquet, on the day of the weaning of Isaac.

text.NRSV: But Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, playing with her son Isaac.
text.Rotherham: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian woman whom she had borne to Abraham laughing!

text.NRSV: So she said to Abraham, “Cast out this slave woman with her son; for the son of this slave woman shall not inherit along with my son Isaac.”
text.Rotherham: So she said to Abraham, Cast out this bondwoman and her son,—for the son of this bondwoman must not inherit with my son with Isaac.

text.NRSV: The matter was very distressing to Abraham on account of his son.
text.Rotherham: And the word was very grievous in the eyes of Abraham,—on account of his son.

text.NRSV: But God said to Abraham, “Do not be distressed because of the boy and because of your slave woman; whatever Sarah says to you, do as she tells you, for it is through Isaac that offspring shall be named for you.
text.Rotherham: And God said unto Abraham Let it not be grievous in thine eyes concerning the boy and concerning thy bondwoman, In all that Sarah may say unto thee, hearken to her voice,—For in Isaac, shall there be called to thee—a seed.

text.NRSV: As for the son of the slave woman, I will make a nation of him also, because he is your offspring.”
text.Rotherham: Yet even the son of the bondwoman, will I appoint to become, a nation, because thy seed, he is.

text.NRSV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water, and gave it to Hagar, putting it on her shoulder, along with the child, and sent her away. And she departed, and wandered about in the wilderness of Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham rose up early in the morning—and took bread and a skin of water and gave unto hagar,—putting them on her shoulder and the child, and sent her forth,—so she went her way and wandered, in the desert of Beer-sheba.

text.NRSV: When the water in the skin was gone, she cast the child under one of the bushes.
text.Rotherham: And the water out of the skin was spent,—so she cast the child under one of the shrubs;

text.NRSV: Then she went and sat down opposite him a good way off, about the distance of a bowshot; for she said, “Do not let me look on the death of the child.” And as she sat opposite him, she lifted up her voice and wept.
text.Rotherham: and went and sat her down over against him at a distance like as of such as draw the bow, for she said Let me not look upon the death of the child,—So she sat down over against him, and the boy lifted up his voice, and wept

text.NRSV: And God heard the voice of the boy; and the angel of God called to Hagar from heaven, and said to her, “What troubles you, Hagar? Do not be afraid; for God has heard the voice of the boy where he is.
text.Rotherham: And God heard the voice of the boy, and a messenger of God called unto Hagar out of the heavens; and said to her, What aileth thee, Hagar? Do not fear, for God hath hearkened unto the voice of the boy where he is.

text.NRSV: Come, lift up the boy and hold him fast with your hand, for I will make a great nation of him.”
text.Rotherham: Arise lift up the boy, and hold him up with thy hand,—for, a great nation, will I make him.

text.NRSV: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water. She went, and filled the skin with water, and gave the boy a drink.
text.Rotherham: And God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water,—and went and filled the skin with water, and gave drink unto the boy

text.NRSV: God was with the boy, and he grew up; he lived in the wilderness, and became an expert with the bow.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that God was with the boy, and he grew,—and dwelt in the desert, and he became as he grew up, an archer,

text.NRSV: He lived in the wilderness of Paran; and his mother got a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And he dwelt in the desert of Paran,—and his mother took him a wife out of the land of Egypt.

text.NRSV: At that time Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, said to Abraham, “God is with you in all that you do;
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass at that time, that Abimelech, with Phicol chieftain of his host, spake unto Abraham, saying,—God, is with thee in all that, thou art doing.

text.NRSV: now therefore swear to me here by God that you will not deal falsely with me or with my offspring or with my posterity, but as I have dealt loyally with you, you will deal with me and with the land where you have resided as an alien.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, swear to me by God, here, that thou wilt not deal falsely by me, nor by mine offspring, nor by my descendants,—according to the lovingkindness wherewith I have dealt with thee, shalt thou deal with me, and with the land wherein thou hast sojourned.

text.NRSV: And Abraham said, “I swear it.”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, I, will swear.

text.NRSV: When Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well of water that Abimelech’s servants had seized,
text.Rotherham: But Abraham reproved Abimelech,—on account of the well of water, which the servants of Abimelech had seized.

text.NRSV: Abimelech said, “I do not know who has done this; you did not tell me, and I have not heard of it until today.”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said, I know not, who hath done this thing,—nor hast even thou, ever told me, nor have even I, ever heard, save to-day.

text.NRSV: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two men made a covenant.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham took sheep and oxen, and gave to Abimelech,—and they two solemnised a covenant

text.NRSV: Abraham set apart seven ewe lambs of the flock.
text.Rotherham: So then Abraham set seven young sheep of the flock by them-selves,

text.NRSV: And Abimelech said to Abraham, “What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs that you have set apart?”
text.Rotherham: And Abimelech said unto Abraham, What, then mean these seven young sheep here, which thou hast set by themselves?

text.NRSV: He said, “These seven ewe lambs you shall accept from my hand, in order that you may be a witness for me that I dug this well.”
text.Rotherham: And he said, The seven young sheep, shalt thou take at my hand,—that they may serve as my witness, that I digged this well.

text.NRSV: Therefore that place was called Beer-sheba; because there both of them swore an oath.
text.Rotherham: On this account, was the name of that place called Beer-sheba [="oath well"],—because there, had they two sworn.

text.NRSV: When they had made a covenant at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, with Phicol the commander of his army, left and returned to the land of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: And when they had solemnised a covenant in Beer-sheba,—then arose Abimelech, with Phicol chief of his host, and returned unto the land of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and called there on the name of the Lord, the Everlasting God.
text.Rotherham: Then planted he a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba,—and called there on the name of Yahweh the age-abiding GOD.

text.NRSV: And Abraham resided as an alien many days in the land of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.

text.NRSV: After these things God tested Abraham. He said to him, “Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that God, did prove Abraham,—and he said unto him, Abraham! And he said Behold me!

text.NRSV: He said, “Take your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains that I shall show you.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Take, I pray thee, thy son, thine only one, whom thou lovest, even, Isaac, and get thee into the land of Moriah and cause him to ascend there as an ascending-sacrifice, on one of the mountains which I shall name unto thee.

text.NRSV: So Abraham rose early in the morning, saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, and his son Isaac; he cut the wood for the burnt offering, and set out and went to the place in the distance that God had shown him.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and saddled his ass, and took his two young men with him, and Isaac his son, and clave the pieces of wood for an ascending-sacrifice, and mounted and went his way unto the place which God had named to him.

text.NRSV: On the third day Abraham looked up and saw the place far away.
text.Rotherham: It was, on the third day, that Abraham lifted up his eyes, and saw the place afar off.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham said to his young men, “Stay here with the donkey; the boy and I will go over there; we will worship, and then we will come back to you.”
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said unto his young men—Tarry by yourselves here with the ass, but I and the young man must go yonder,—that we may bow ourselves down and return unto you.

text.NRSV: Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on his son Isaac, and he himself carried the fire and the knife. So the two of them walked on together.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham took the pieces of wood for the ascending-sacrifice, and laid them on Isaac his son, and took in his own hand the fire and the knife, and they went on their way, both of them together.

text.NRSV: Isaac said to his father Abraham, “Father!” And he said, “Here I am, my son.” He said, “The fire and the wood are here, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaac unto Abraham his father, then said he; My father! And he said, Behold me, my son, And he said, Behold the fire, and the pieces of wood,—but where is the lamb, for an ascending-sacrifice?

text.NRSV: Abraham said, “God himself will provide the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.” So the two of them walked on together.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham said, God, will provide for himself the lamb for an ascending-sacrifice, my son! So they went on their way, both of them, together.

text.NRSV: When they came to the place that God had shown him, Abraham built an altar there and laid the wood in order. He bound his son Isaac, and laid him on the altar, on top of the wood.
text.Rotherham: Then came they into the place which God had named to him, and Abraham built there the altar, and laid in order the pieces of wood, and bound Isaac his son, and laid him on the altar, above the pieces of wood.

text.NRSV: Then Abraham reached out his hand and took the knife to kill his son.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham put forth his hand, and took the knife,—to slay his son.

text.NRSV: But the angel of the Lord called to him from heaven, and said, “Abraham, Abraham!” And he said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: Then called out unto him the messenger of Yahweh out of the heavens, and said. Abraham, Abraham! And he said Behold me!

text.NRSV: He said, “Do not lay your hand on the boy or do anything to him; for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from me.”
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Do not put forth thy hand unto the young man, neither do to him—anything at all,—for, now, know I that one who reverest God, thou art, when thou hast not withheld thy son, thine only one, from me.

text.NRSV: And Abraham looked up and saw a ram, caught in a thicket by its horns. Abraham went and took the ram and offered it up as a burnt offering instead of his son.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham lifted up his eyes and looked, and lo! a ram, behind, caught in a thicket by his horns, and Abraham went, and took the ram, and caused him to ascend as an ascending-sacrifice instead of his son.

text.NRSV: So Abraham called that place “The Lord will provide”; as it is said to this day, “On the mount of the Lord it shall be provided.”
text.Rotherham: So Abraham called the name of that place Yahweh-yireh,—as to which it is still said to-day, In the mountain of Yahweh, will provision be made.

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord called to Abraham a second time from heaven,
text.Rotherham: Then called out the messenger of Yahweh unto Abraham,—a second time out of the heavens;

text.NRSV: and said, “By myself I have sworn, says the Lord: Because you have done this, and have not withheld your son, your only son,
text.Rotherham: and said, By myself, have I sworn is the oracle of Yahweh,—Because thou hast done this thing, and hast not withheld thy son, thine only one,

text.NRSV: I will indeed bless you, and I will make your offspring as numerous as the stars of heaven and as the sand that is on the seashore. And your offspring shall possess the gate of their enemies,
text.Rotherham: That I will, richly bless, thee, and, abundantly multiply, thy seed, as the stars of the heavens, and as the sand which is on the lip of the sea,—that thy seed may take possession of the gate of his foes:

text.NRSV: and by your offspring shall all the nations of the earth gain blessing for themselves, because you have obeyed my voice.”
text.Rotherham: So shall all the nations of the earth bless themselves in thy seed,—Because thou didst hearken unto my voice.

text.NRSV: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they arose and went together to Beer-sheba; and Abraham lived at Beer-sheba.
text.Rotherham: So Abraham returned unto his young men, and they mounted, and went on their way together unto Beer-sheba,—and Abraham dwelt in Beer-sheba.

text.NRSV: Now after these things it was told Abraham, “Milcah also has borne children, to your brother Nahor:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that it was told Abraham, saying,—Behold! Milcah—she also, hath borne sons, to Nahor thy brother:

text.NRSV: Uz the firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,
text.Rotherham: Uz his firstborn and Buz his brother,—and Kemuel, father of Aram—

text.NRSV: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.”
text.Rotherham: and Chesed and Hazo, and Pildash, and Jidlaph,—and Bethuel;

text.NRSV: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham’s brother.
text.Rotherham: And, Bethuel, hath begotten Rebekah,—These eight, hath Milcah borne to Nahor brother of Abraham.

text.NRSV: Moreover, his concubine, whose name was Reumah, bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text.Rotherham: And his concubine, whose name was Reumah, she also, hath borne Tebah and Gaham, and Tahash, and Maacah.

text.NRSV: Sarah lived one hundred twenty-seven years; this was the length of Sarah’s life.
text.Rotherham: And the life of Sarah came to be, a hundred and twenty-seven years,—the years of the life of Sarah.

text.NRSV: And Sarah died at Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan; and Abraham went in to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
text.Rotherham: And Sarah died in Kiriath-arba, the same, is Hebron in the land of Canaan,—and Abraham went in to wail for Sarah and to weep for her.

text.NRSV: Abraham rose up from beside his dead, and said to the Hittites,
text.Rotherham: And Abraham rose up from over the face of his dead,—and spake unto the sons of Heth, saying;

text.NRSV: “I am a stranger and an alien residing among you; give me property among you for a burying place, so that I may bury my dead out of my sight.”
text.Rotherham: A sojourner and settler, am I with you,—Give me a possession of a buryingplace with you, That I may bury my dead, from before me.

text.NRSV: The Hittites answered Abraham,
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him:

text.NRSV: “Hear us, my lord; you are a mighty prince among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our burial places; none of us will withhold from you any burial ground for burying your dead.”
text.Rotherham: Hear us, my lord! A prince of God, art thou in our midst, In the choice of our buryingplaces, bury thy dead,—Not, a man from among us his, burying-place, will withhold from thee, from burying thy dead.

text.NRSV: Abraham rose and bowed to the Hittites, the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: And Abraham rose up, and bowed himself down to the people of the land, to the sons of Heth;
