text.Rotherham: Then said Israel unto Joseph I am ready to die at last,—now that I have seen thy face, in that thou art, yet alive.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his brothers and to his father’s household, “I will go up and tell Pharaoh, and will say to him, ‘My brothers and my father’s household, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren and unto the house of his father, I must go up, and must tell Pharaoh—and must say unto him, My brethren, and the house of my father, who were in the land of Canaan, have come in unto me.

text.NRSV: The men are shepherds, for they have been keepers of livestock; and they have brought their flocks, and their herds, and all that they have.’
text.Rotherham: Now, the men, are feeders of flocks, for men of cattle, they are,—and their flocks and their herds and all that they have, have they brought in.

text.NRSV: When Pharaoh calls you, and says, ‘What is your occupation?’
text.Rotherham: So shall it come to pass, when Pharaoh shall call for you,—and shall say—What is your occupation?

text.NRSV: you shall say, ‘Your servants have been keepers of livestock from our youth even until now, both we and our ancestors’—in order that you may settle in the land of Goshen, because all shepherds are abhorrent to the Egyptians.”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye say—Men of cattle, have thy servants been from our youth even until now, both we and our fathers,—To the end ye may dwell in the land of Goshen, for an abomination to Egyptians, is every feeder of a flock,

text.NRSV: So Joseph went and told Pharaoh, “My father and my brothers, with their flocks and herds and all that they possess, have come from the land of Canaan; they are now in the land of Goshen.”
text.Rotherham: So then Joseph went in, and told Pharaoh, and said—My father and my brethren, and their flocks and their herds and all that they have, are come in from the land of Canaan,—and, here they are, in the land of Goshen.

text.NRSV: From among his brothers he took five men and presented them to Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: Moreover out of the whole number of his brethren, he took with him five men,—and set them before Pharaoh.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh said to his brothers, “What is your occupation?” And they said to Pharaoh, “Your servants are shepherds, as our ancestors were.”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said unto the brethren of Joseph—What is your occupation? And they said unto Pharaoh—Feeders of flocks, are thy servants, both we and our fathers.

text.NRSV: They said to Pharaoh, “We have come to reside as aliens in the land; for there is no pasture for your servants’ flocks because the famine is severe in the land of Canaan. Now, we ask you, let your servants settle in the land of Goshen.”
text.Rotherham: And they said unto Pharaoh—To sojourn in the land, are we come in, for there is no pasture for the flocks which pertain to thy servants, for, severe, is the famine in the land of Canaan. Now, therefore, let thy servants dwell we pray thee in the land of Goshen.

text.NRSV: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Your father and your brothers have come to you.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Pharaoh unto Joseph saying,—Thy father and thy brethren, are come in unto thee.

text.NRSV: The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and your brothers in the best region of the land; let them live in the land of Goshen; and if you know that there are capable men among them, put them in charge of my livestock.”
text.Rotherham: The land of Egypt, is, before thee, in the best of the land, cause thou thy father and thy brethren to dwell,—let them dwell in the land of Goshen, And if thou knowest that there are among them men of ability, then shalt thou set them as chieftains of cattle over mine.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph brought in his father Jacob, and presented him to Pharaoh, and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph brought in Jacob his father, and caused him to stand before Pharaoh,—and Jacob blessed Pharaoh.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh said to Jacob, “How many are the years of your life?”
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said unto Jacob: About how many, have been the days of the years of thy life?

text.NRSV: Jacob said to Pharaoh, “The years of my earthly sojourn are one hundred thirty; few and hard have been the years of my life. They do not compare with the years of the life of my ancestors during their long sojourn.”
text.Rotherham: And Jacob said unto Pharaoh, The days of the years of my sojournings, have been a hundred and thirty years,—Few and evil, have been the days of the years of my life, neither have they attained unto the days of the years of the lives of my fathers, in the days of their sojournings.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob blessed Pharaoh, and went out from the presence of Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob blessed Pharaoh,—and came forth from the presence of Pharaoh.

text.NRSV: Joseph settled his father and his brothers, and granted them a holding in the land of Egypt, in the best part of the land, in the land of Rameses, as Pharaoh had instructed.
text.Rotherham: So then Joseph fixed the dwelling of his father and his brethren, and gave them a possession in the land of Egypt in the best of the land. in the land of Rameses,—as Pharaoh had commanded.

text.NRSV: And Joseph provided his father, his brothers, and all his father’s household with food, according to the number of their dependents.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph nourished his father and his brethren, and all his father’s house,—with bread, according to the need of their little ones.

text.NRSV: Now there was no food in all the land, for the famine was very severe. The land of Egypt and the land of Canaan languished because of the famine.
text.Rotherham: But, bread, was there none in all the land,—for the famine was, very severe,—so that the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan fainted by reason of the famine,

text.NRSV: Joseph collected all the money to be found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, in exchange for the grain that they bought; and Joseph brought the money into Pharaoh’s house.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph gathered up all the silver that was found in the land of Egypt. and in the land of Canaan, for the corn which, they, were buying,—and Joseph brought the silver into the house of Pharaoh.

text.NRSV: When the money from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan was spent, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, and said, “Give us food! Why should we die before your eyes? For our money is gone.”
text.Rotherham: And the silver was spent out of the land of Egypt and out of the land of Canaan, so all the Egyptians came in unto Joseph, saying—Do give us bread, for why should we die before thee, although, silver, hath failed?

text.NRSV: And Joseph answered, “Give me your livestock, and I will give you food in exchange for your livestock, if your money is gone.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph: Give your cattle, and I will give you for your cattle,—if silver hath failed.

text.NRSV: So they brought their livestock to Joseph; and Joseph gave them food in exchange for the horses, the flocks, the herds, and the donkeys. That year he supplied them with food in exchange for all their livestock.
text.Rotherham: So they brought in their cattle unto Joseph, and Joseph gave them bread in exchange for the horses and for the cattle of the flock and for the cattle of the herd, and for the asses,—and he provided them with bread for all their cattle, throughout that year.

text.NRSV: When that year was ended, they came to him the following year, and said to him, “We can hide nothing from my lord: our money is all spent; and the herds of cattle are my lord’s. There is nothing left in the sight of my lord but our bodies and our lands.
text.Rotherham: When that year was ended, then came they unto him in the second year and said to him—We will not hide from my lord, how that the silver and the herds of beasts for my lord are come to an end,—there is nothing left before my lord, save only our bodies and our ground:

text.NRSV: Shall we die before your eyes, both we and our land? Buy us and our land in exchange for food. We with our land will become slaves to Pharaoh; just give us seed, so that we may live and not die, and that the land may not become desolate.”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore should we die before thine eyes both we, and our ground? Buy thou us and our ground, for bread,—and we and our ground will become servants to Pharaoh, and give seed, that we may live, and not die, and the ground, not lie waste.

text.NRSV: So Joseph bought all the land of Egypt for Pharaoh. All the Egyptians sold their fields, because the famine was severe upon them; and the land became Pharaoh’s.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph bought all the ground of the Egyptians for Pharaoh, for the Egyptians sold every man his field, because the famine had laid fast hold upon them,—so the land became Pharaoh’s.

text.NRSV: As for the people, he made slaves of them from one end of Egypt to the other.
text.Rotherham: And as for the people, he brought them into bondage as servants,—from one end of the boundary of Egypt unto the other end thereof.

text.NRSV: Only the land of the priests he did not buy; for the priests had a fixed allowance from Pharaoh, and lived on the allowance that Pharaoh gave them; therefore they did not sell their land.
text.Rotherham: Only, the ground of the priests, bought he not,—for the priests had a statutory-portion, from Pharaoh and they had been eating their statutory-portion, which Pharaoh had given them, for which cause, they had not sold their ground.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph said to the people, “Now that I have this day bought you and your land for Pharaoh, here is seed for you; sow the land.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto the people, Lo! I have bought you this day, and your ground for Pharaoh,—see, here is seed for you, so shall ye sow the ground;

text.NRSV: And at the harvests you shall give one-fifth to Pharaoh, and four-fifths shall be your own, as seed for the field and as food for yourselves and your households, and as food for your little ones.”
text.Rotherham: and it shall come to pass that, of the yield, ye shall give a fifth to Pharaoh,—but the four parts, shall be your own—for seed for the field, and for your food and for them who are in your households and for food for your little ones.

text.NRSV: They said, “You have saved our lives; may it please my lord, we will be slaves to Pharaoh.”
text.Rotherham: And they said—Thou hast saved our lives! let us find favour in the eyes of my lord, so will we become Pharaoh’s servants.

text.NRSV: So Joseph made it a statute concerning the land of Egypt, and it stands to this day, that Pharaoh should have the fifth. The land of the priests alone did not become Pharaoh’s.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph appointed it for a statute—until this day, regarding the ground of Egypt, that Pharaoh, should take a fifth,—only, the ground of the priests, was, theirs alone, it had not become Pharaoh’s.

text.NRSV: Thus Israel settled in the land of Egypt, in the region of Goshen; and they gained possessions in it, and were fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.
text.Rotherham: Thus Israel dwelt in the land of Egypt in the land of Goshen,—and gained possessions therein, and became fruitful and multiplied exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years; so the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were one hundred forty-seven years.
text.Rotherham: And Jacob lived in the land of Egypt seventeen years,—so it came to pass that the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were a hundred and forty-seven years.

text.NRSV: When the time of Israel’s death drew near, he called his son Joseph and said to him, “If I have found favor with you, put your hand under my thigh and promise to deal loyally and truly with me. Do not bury me in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And the days of Israel drew near that he must die, so he called for his son for Joseph and said to him—If, I pray thee I have found favour in thine eyes, place, I pray thee thy hand under my thigh,—so shalt thou deal with me in lovingkindness and faithfulness—Do not I pray thee, bury me in Egypt.

text.NRSV: When I lie down with my ancestors, carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burial place.” He answered, “I will do as you have said.”
text.Rotherham: When I shall lie down with my fathers, then shalt thou carry me out of Egypt, and bury me in their buryingplace. And he said: I, will do according to thy word.

text.NRSV: And he said, “Swear to me”; and he swore to him. Then Israel bowed himself on the head of his bed.
text.Rotherham: Then he said: Come, swear to me! And he sware to him. Then did Israel bow himself down on the head of the couch.

text.NRSV: After this Joseph was told, “Your father is ill.” So he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass after these things, that one said to Joseph, Lo! thy father, is sick. So he took his two sons with him, Manasseh and Ephraim.

text.NRSV: When Jacob was told, “Your son Joseph has come to you,” he summoned his strength and sat up in bed.
text.Rotherham: And one told Jacob, and said—Lo! thy son Joseph, coming in unto thee. So Israel strengthened himself, and sat up on the couch.

text.NRSV: And Jacob said to Joseph, “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and he blessed me,
text.Rotherham: Then said Jacob unto Joseph, GOD Almighty, appeared unto me in Luz in the land of Canaan,—and blessed me;

text.NRSV: and said to me, ‘I am going to make you fruitful and increase your numbers; I will make of you a company of peoples, and will give this land to your offspring after you for a perpetual holding.’
text.Rotherham: and said unto me—Behold me! about to make thee fruitful, so will I multiply thee, and give thee to become a multitude of peoples,—so will I give this land to thy seed after thee, as an age-abiding possession.

text.NRSV: Therefore your two sons, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are now mine; Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, just as Reuben and Simeon are.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore thy two sons who were born to thee in the land of Egypt, before I came in unto thee in Egypt, are mine! Ephraim and Manasseh, like Reuben and Simeon, shall be mine!

text.NRSV: Any children that you beget after them shall be yours; they shall be called by the name of their brothers in their inheritance.
text.Rotherham: But thine offspring which thou hast begotten after them, thine own shall they be,—after the name of their brethren, shall they be called in their inheritance.

text.NRSV: For when I came from Paddan, Rachel, alas, died in the land of Canaan on the way, while there was still some distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem).”
text.Rotherham: But, as for me, when I came in from Padan, Rachel died by me, in the land of Canaan, in the way, while yet there was a stretch of country to come into Ephrath,—so I buried her there in the way to Ephrath, the same, is Bethlehem.

text.NRSV: When Israel saw Joseph’s sons, he said, “Who are these?”
text.Rotherham: Then Israel saw Joseph’s sons,—and said—Who are these?

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his father, “They are my sons, whom God has given me here.” And he said, “Bring them to me, please, that I may bless them.”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto his father, My sons, they are, whom God hath given me, in this place. And he said—Bring them, I pray thee, unto me, that I may bless them.

text.NRSV: Now the eyes of Israel were dim with age, and he could not see well. So Joseph brought them near him; and he kissed them and embraced them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the eyes of Israel, had become dim from old age,—he could not see,—so he drew them near unto him, and kissed them, and embraced them.

text.NRSV: Israel said to Joseph, “I did not expect to see your face; and here God has let me see your children also.”
text.Rotherham: And Israel said unto Joseph, To see thine own face, had I not thought,—and lo! God hath caused me to see even thy seed!

text.NRSV: Then Joseph removed them from his father’s knees, and he bowed himself with his face to the earth.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph brought them out from beside his knees, and bowed himself down with his face towards the earth.

text.NRSV: Joseph took them both, Ephraim in his right hand toward Israel’s left, and Manasseh in his left hand toward Israel’s right, and brought them near him.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph took them both,—Ephraim in his right hand on Israel’s left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand, on Israel’s right hand, and drew them near unto him.

text.NRSV: But Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Ephraim, who was the younger, and his left hand on the head of Manasseh, crossing his hands, for Manasseh was the firstborn.
text.Rotherham: Then Israel put forth his right hand and laid it upon the head of Ephraim, though, he, was the younger, and his left hand upon the head of Manasseh,—crossing his hands, although, Manasseh, was the first-born.

text.NRSV: He blessed Joseph, and said, “The God before whom my ancestors Abraham and Isaac walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life to this day,
text.Rotherham: Thus blessed he Joseph, and said,—God himself—before whom walked my fathers. Abraham and Isaac, God himself—who hath been my shepherd, since I came into being until this day;—

text.NRSV: the angel who has redeemed me from all harm, bless the boys; and in them let my name be perpetuated, and the name of my ancestors Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a multitude on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: The Messenger—who hath been my redeemer from all evil—bless the lads! And may there be called upon them, my name, and the name of my fathers. Abraham and Isaac; And may they swarm into a multitude, in the midst of the earth!

text.NRSV: When Joseph saw that his father laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim, it displeased him; so he took his father’s hand, to remove it from Ephraim’s head to Manasseh’s head.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph saw that his father had laid his right hand on the head of Ephraim and it was displeasing in his eyes,—so he took hold of his father’s hand to remove it from off the head of Ephraim, on to the head of Manasseh.

text.NRSV: Joseph said to his father, “Not so, my father! Since this one is the firstborn, put your right hand on his head.”
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto his father—Not so, my father! for, this, is the firstborn, lay thy right hand upon his head.

text.NRSV: But his father refused, and said, “I know, my son, I know; he also shall become a people, and he also shall be great. Nevertheless his younger brother shall be greater than he, and his offspring shall become a multitude of nations.”
text.Rotherham: And his father refused, and said—I know—my son, I know! He also, shall become a people, and he also, shall become great,—but nevertheless, his younger brother, shall become greater than he, and his seed, shall become a filling up of the nations.

text.NRSV: So he blessed them that day, saying, “By you Israel will invoke blessings, saying, ‘God make you like Ephraim and like Manasseh.’” So he put Ephraim ahead of Manasseh.
text.Rotherham: And he blessed them on that day, saying—In thee, let Israel bless himself saying, God set thee as Ephraim, and as Manasseh! So he set Ephraim before Manasseh.

text.NRSV: Then Israel said to Joseph, “I am about to die, but God will be with you and will bring you again to the land of your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: And Israel said unto Joseph: Lo! I, am about to die,—but it shall come to pass that, God will be with you, and will take you back into the land of your fathers.

text.NRSV: I now give to you one portion more than to your brothers, the portion that I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and with my bow.”
text.Rotherham: But, I, have given to thee one mountain-track above thy brethren,—which I took out of the hand of the Amorite, with my sword and with my bow.

text.NRSV: Then Jacob called his sons, and said: “Gather together, that I may tell you what is to happen to you in days to come.
text.Rotherham: Then called Jacob unto his sons,—and said—Gather yourselves together, and let me tell you, that which shall befall you in the afterpart of the days.

text.NRSV: Assemble and hear, O sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.
text.Rotherham: Assemble yourselves and hear ye sons of Jacob,—And hearken unto Israel your father!

text.NRSV: Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might and the first fruits of my vigor, excelling in rank and excelling in power.
text.Rotherham: Reuben, my first-born, thou, My vigour, and the first-fruit of my strength,—Pre-eminence of elevation and pre-eminence of power:

text.NRSV: Unstable as water, you shall no longer excel because you went up onto your father’s bed; then you defiled it—you went up onto my couch!
text.Rotherham: Boiling over like water, thou mayest not have pre-eminence, Because thou didst mount the bed of thy father,—Then, wast thou profane—My marriage-bed, he mounted!

text.NRSV: Simeon and Levi are brothers; weapons of violence are their swords.
text.Rotherham: Simeon and Levi, are brethren,—Instruments of violence, are their agreements:

text.NRSV: May I never come into their council; may I not be joined to their company—for in their anger they kill men, and at their whim they hamstrung oxen.
text.Rotherham: Into their circle, do not thou enter my soul! In their convocation, do not thou join mine honour! For in their anger, they slew brave men, And in their wantonness, they ham-strung oxen.

text.NRSV: Cursed be their anger, for it is fierce, and their wrath, for it is cruel! I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.
text.Rotherham: Accursed, be their anger, because fierce, And their wrath because cruel,—I divide them in Jacob, And I disperse them in Israel.

text.NRSV: Judah, your brothers shall praise you; your hand shall be on the neck of your enemies; your father’s sons shall bow down before you.
text.Rotherham: Judah, as for thee, thy brethren shall praise thee when thy hand is on the neck of thy foes,—The sons of thy father, shall bow them-selves down to thee:

text.NRSV: Judah is a lion’s whelp; from the prey, my son, you have gone up. He couches down, he stretches out like a lion, like a lioness—who dares rouse him up?
text.Rotherham: A lion’s whelp, is Judah, From the prey, my son hast thou come up! He hath stooped—hath crouched As a lion—or as a lioness, Who shall rouse him?

text.NRSV: The scepter shall not depart from Judah, nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until tribute comes to him; and the obedience of the peoples is his.
text.Rotherham: The sceptre shall not depart from Judah, Nor the commander’s staff from between his feet,—Until that he come in as a Shiloh, And, his, be the obedience of the peoples:

text.NRSV: Binding his foal to the vine and his donkey’s colt to the choice vine, he washes his garments in wine and his robe in the blood of grapes;
text.Rotherham: Binding, to the vine, his ass, And to the choice vine his ass’s colt, He hath washed in wine his raiment, And in the blood of grapes, his mantle:

text.NRSV: his eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
text.Rotherham: Darker—his eyes, than wine,—Whiter—his teeth than milk!

text.NRSV: Zebulun shall settle at the shore of the sea; he shall be a haven for ships, and his border shall be at Sidon.
text.Rotherham: Zebulon, by a haven of seas, shall he settle down,—Even he, by a haven of ships, With his utmost part upon Zidon.

text.NRSV: Issachar is a strong donkey, lying down between the sheepfolds;
text.Rotherham: Issachar, an ass of strength,—couching between the pens;

text.NRSV: he saw that a resting place was good, and that the land was pleasant; so he bowed his shoulder to the burden, and became a slave at forced labor.
text.Rotherham: Then beholdeth he rest, that it is good, And the ground that it is pleasant,—So he bendeth his shoulder to bear, And becometh a tributary servant.

text.NRSV: Dan shall judge his people as one of the tribes of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Dan, shall vindicate his people,—as one of the tribes of Israel;

text.NRSV: Dan shall be a snake by the roadside, a viper along the path, that bites the horse’s heels so that its rider falls backward.
text.Rotherham: It cometh to pass that, Dan, is a serpent upon the way, A horned viper, upon the path,—That biteth the heels of the horse, And his rider falleth backwards:—

text.NRSV: I wait for your salvation, O Lord.
text.Rotherham: For thy salvation, have I waited O Yahweh!

text.NRSV: Gad shall be raided by raiders, but he shall raid at their heels.
text.Rotherham: Gad! a troop shall troop on him,—But, he, shall troop on the rear.

text.NRSV: Asher’s food shall be rich, and he shall provide royal delicacies.
text.Rotherham: Out of Asher, fatness, is his food,—So, he, setteth forth dainties for a king.

text.NRSV: Naphtali is a doe let loose that bears lovely fawns.
text.Rotherham: Naphtali, is a slender hind,—That putteth forth antlers of beauty.

text.NRSV: Joseph is a fruitful bough, a fruitful bough by a spring; his branches run over the wall.
text.Rotherham: A fruitful bough, is Joseph, A fruitful bough over a fountain,—Branches creeping over a wall.

text.NRSV: The archers fiercely attacked him; they shot at him and pressed him hard.
text.Rotherham: So they attack him and have shot,—So they enclose him, do the masters of arrows.

text.NRSV: Yet his bow remained taut, and his arms were made agile by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, by the name of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel,
text.Rotherham: But abideth, as an enduring one, his bow, And supple are the arms of his hands,—From the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, From thence, is the Shepherd the Stone of Israel:

text.NRSV: by the God of your father, who will help you, by the Almighty who will bless you with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lies beneath, blessings of the breasts and of the womb.
text.Rotherham: From the GOD of thy father who doth help thee And GOD Almighty who doth bless thee, Blessings of the heavens, above, Blessings of the abyss, couching beneath,—Blessings of breasts and womb:

text.NRSV: The blessings of your father are stronger than the blessings of the eternal mountains, the bounties of the everlasting hills; may they be on the head of Joseph, on the brow of him who was set apart from his brothers.
text.Rotherham: The blessings of thy father, have prevailed, Over the blessings of the perpetual mountains, The charm of the age-abiding hills,—They turn out to be, for the head of Joseph, And for the crown of the head of him who was set apart from his brethren.

text.NRSV: Benjamin is a ravenous wolf, in the morning devouring the prey, and at evening dividing the spoil.”
text.Rotherham: Benjamin a wolf that teareth in pieces, In the morning, he eateth prey, And at eventide, he divideth spoil.

text.NRSV: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said to them when he blessed them, blessing each one of them with a suitable blessing.
text.Rotherham: All these, are the twelve tribes of Israel,—And this, is that which their father spake to them when he blessed them, Each man severally according to the blessing wherewith he blessed them.

text.NRSV: Then he charged them, saying to them, “I am about to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my ancestors—in the cave in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
text.Rotherham: Then commanded he them and said unto them—I, am about to be gathered unto my people, Bury me among my fathers,—within the cave, that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite:

text.NRSV: in the cave in the field at Machpelah, near Mamre, in the land of Canaan, in the field that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite as a burial site.
text.Rotherham: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah which is over against Mamre, in the land of Canaan,—which Abraham bought, along with the field, from Ephron the Hittite for a possession of a buryingplace:—

text.NRSV: There Abraham and his wife Sarah were buried; there Isaac and his wife Rebekah were buried; and there I buried Leah—
text.Rotherham: There, buried they Abraham, and Sarah his wife, There, buried they Isaac, and Rebekah his wife,—And there, buried I Leah:—

text.NRSV: the field and the cave that is in it were purchased from the Hittites.”
text.Rotherham: buying the field and the cave that is therein, from the sons of Heth.

text.NRSV: When Jacob ended his charge to his sons, he drew up his feet into the bed, breathed his last, and was gathered to his people.
text.Rotherham: So Jacob made an end of commanding his sons, and gathered up his feet into the couch,—and ceased to breathe and was gathered unto his people.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph threw himself on his father's face and wept over him and kissed him.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph fell upon the face of his father, and wept upon him and kissed him.

text.NRSV: Joseph commanded the physicians in his service to embalm his father. So the physicians embalmed Israel;
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph commanded his servants the physicians, to embalm his father,—so the physicians embalmed Israel.

text.NRSV: they spent forty days in doing this, for that is the time required for embalming. And the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.
text.Rotherham: And they fulfilled for him forty days, for so, are they wont to fulfil the days of the embalmed,—and the Egyptians wept for him seventy days.

text.NRSV: When the days of weeping for him were past, Joseph addressed the household of Pharaoh, "If now I have found favor with you, please speak to Pharaoh as follows:
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of weeping for him were passed, Joseph spake unto the house of Pharaoh saying,—If I pray you, I have found favour in your eyes, speak ye I pray you in the ears of Pharaoh saying:

text.NRSV: My father made me swear an oath; he said, 'I am about to die. In the tomb that I hewed out for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me.' Now therefore let me go up, so that I may bury my father; then I will return."
text.Rotherham: My father, made me swear saying,—Lo! I, am about to die,—in my grave which I digged for myself in the land of Canaan, there, shalt thou bury me. Now, therefore, let me go up, I pray thee and bury my father, and return.

text.NRSV: Pharaoh answered, "Go up, and bury your father, as he made you swear to do."
text.Rotherham: And Pharaoh said,—Go up., and bury thy father according as he made thee swear.

text.NRSV: So Joseph went up to bury his father. With him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his household, and all the elders of the land of Egypt,
text.Rotherham: So Joseph went up. to bury his father,—and there went up with him all the servants of Pharaoh the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt;

text.NRSV: as well as all the household of Joseph, his brothers, and his father's household. Only their children, their flocks, and their herds were left in the land of Goshen.
text.Rotherham: and all the house of Joseph, and his brethren, and the house of his father,—only their little ones and their flocks and their herds, left they. in the land of Goshen.

text.NRSV: Both chariots and charioteers went up with him. It was a very great company.
text.Rotherham: And there went up with him, both chariots and horsemen,—so it came to pass that, the company, was exceeding great,

text.NRSV: When they came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they held there a very great and sorrowful lamentation; and he observed a time of mourning for his father seven days.
text.Rotherham: And they came in, as far as the threshing-floor of the Buckthorn, which is beyond the Jordan, then wailed they there—an exceeding great and grievous wailing,—and he made for his father a mourning, of seven days.

text.NRSV: When the Canaanite inhabitants of the land saw the mourning on the threshing floor of Atad, they said, "This is a grievous mourning on the part of the Egyptians." Therefore the place was named Abel-mizraim; it is beyond the Jordan.
text.Rotherham: And when the dwellers in the land—the Canaanites—saw the mourning in the threshing-floor of the Buckthorn, they said,—A grievous mourning, this! for the Egyptians. For this cause, was the name thereof called Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.

text.NRSV: Thus his sons did for him as he had instructed them.
text.Rotherham: And his sons did for him thus as he had commanded them;

text.NRSV: They carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field at Machpelah, the field near Mamre, which Abraham bought as a burial site from Ephron the Hittite.
text.Rotherham: so his sons carried him to the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah,—which Abraham bought—with the field—for a possession of a buryingplace from Ephron the Hittite over against Mamre.

text.NRSV: After he had buried his father, Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and all who had gone up with him to bury his father.
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph returned to Egypt he and his brethren and all who had been up with him to bury his father,—after he had buried his father.

text.NRSV: Realizing that their father was dead, Joseph's brothers said, "What if Joseph still bears a grudge against us and pays us back in full for all the wrong that we did to him?"
text.Rotherham: Now when the brethren of Joseph saw that their father was dead, they said—Oh! if Joseph should lie in wait for us,—and should return, to us, all the evil wherewith we requited him!

text.NRSV: So they approached Joseph, saying, "Your father gave this instruction before he died,
text.Rotherham: So they sent in charge unto Joseph saying,—Thy father, gave command before he died saying:

text.NRSV: "Say to Joseph: I beg you, forgive the crime of your brothers and the wrong they did in harming you.' Now therefore please forgive the crime of the servants of the God of your father." Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
text.Rotherham: Thus, shall ye say to Joseph—Ah now! do forgive we pray thee the transgression of thy brethren and their sin in that with evil, they requited thee. Now, therefore grant forgiveness we pray thee, for the transgression of the servants of the God of thy father! And Joseph wept when they spake unto him.

text.NRSV: Then his brothers also wept, fell down before him, and said, "We are here as your slaves."
text.Rotherham: Then went his brethren also, and fell down before him,—and said, Behold us! thine for servants.

text.NRSV: But Joseph said to them, "Do not be afraid! Am I in the place of God?
text.Rotherham: And Joseph said unto them—Do not fear,—although in place of God, I am.

text.NRSV: Even though you intended to do harm to me, God intended it for good, in order to preserve a numerous people, as he is doing today.
text.Rotherham: Ye, indeed, planned against me, evil,—God, planned it, for good, for the sake of doing, as at this day, to save alive much people.

text.NRSV: So have no fear; I myself will provide for you and your little ones." In this way he reassured them, speaking kindly to them.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore do not fear! I will nourish you and your little ones. Thus he consoled them, and spake unto their heart.

text.NRSV: So Joseph remained in Egypt, he and his father's household; and Joseph lived one hundred ten years.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph abode in Egypt, he, and his father’s house,—and Joseph lived a hundred and ten years.

text.NRSV: Joseph saw Ephraim's children of the third generation; the children of Machir son of Manasseh were also born on Joseph's knees.
text.Rotherham: And Joseph saw Ephraim’s, sons of three generations—moreover the sons of Machir, son of Manasseh, were born upon Joseph’s knees.

text.NRSV: Then Joseph said to his brothers, "I am about to die; but God will surely come to you, and bring you up out of this land to the land that he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob."
text.Rotherham: Then said Joseph unto his brethren, I, am about to die,—but God will surely concern, himself for you, so will he take you up out of this land, into the land which he sware to Abraham to Isaac and to Jacob.

text.NRSV: So Joseph made the Israelites swear, saying, "When God comes to you, you shall carry up my bones from here."
text.Rotherham: Then Joseph made the sons of Israel swear saying,—God will, surely concern, himself for you, So shall ye carry up my bones, from hence.

text.NRSV: And Joseph died, being one hundred ten years old; he was embalmed and placed in a coffin in Egypt.
text.Rotherham: So Joseph died, being a hundred and ten years old,—and they embalmed him, and put him in a coffin in Egypt.
